basmala: In the name of God, the All-Merciful, the Compassionate.
intro: Maryam — the sūra of Mary — unfolds a procession of prophets bound by a single grace. It opens on Zachariah who, grown old with a barren wife, called upon his Lord with a secret call (nidāʾan khafiyyan); Qushayrī sees in it the seal of intimacy: he veiled his prayer from creatures so that none might have sight of the secret of his state, and God gave him John, “a name We have given to none before him.” Then comes Mary, who withdraws to a place toward the east: the Spirit appears to her as a perfect man, she conceives and gives birth in solitude — “the pangs of childbirth drove her to the trunk of the palm tree,” and the voice calls: “Shake toward you the trunk of the palm; fresh ripe dates will fall upon you.” The infant Jesus speaks in the cradle: “I am, in truth, the servant of God.” Then Abraham gently pleads with his idolatrous father; Moses, Ishmael, Idrīs pass in turn, and the line of those whom God has graced, “who, when the signs of the All-Merciful were recited to them, fell down prostrate, weeping.” The sūra seals its promise: “the All-Merciful will appoint for those who believe and do righteous deeds love (wuddan)” — Qushayrī: He sets in their hearts a love for Him, the fruit of their pure works, and makes them beloved to the hearts of His creatures and His angels.

versets.ar: كهيعص
versets.tr: kāf hāʾ yāʾ ʿayn ṣād
versets.trad: Kāf. Hā. Yā. ʿAyn. Ṣād.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of these five isolated letters a secret entrusted to the Prophet alone: to the believers belongs the hearing and reciting of them; to the Messenger alone, the grasp of their meaning and their mystery. He reads in them the divine Names: the Kāf points to the One who suffices (al-Kāfī) in bounty and requital; the Hā, to His guidance of the believers toward His knowledge; the Yā, to the ease of His graces after trial, and His hand outstretched in mercy; the ʿAyn, to His knowledge of the servant's states, in secret and in the open; the Ṣād, that He is the Truthful (al-Ṣādiq) in His promise.

mots.sens: Kāf Hā Yā ʿAyn Ṣād
mots.def: the sūra's opening letters, cipher of the divine Names per Qushayrī.

versets.ar: ذِكْرُ رَحْمَتِ رَبِّكَ عَبْدَهُ زَكَرِيَّا
versets.tr: dhikru raḥmati rabbika ʿabdahu zakariyyā
versets.trad: A mention of the mercy of your Lord toward His servant Zachariah,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: this is the mention of the singular favour God showed Zachariah in answering his request for a child, and of the nearness with which He willed to grace his offspring and all his household. The sūra of Mary thus opens on the memory of a grace: before the narrative, the evocation of raḥma, as though every gift proceeded first from the remembrance God keeps of His servant.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / ر ح م / ر ب ب
mots.sens: a mention of your Lord's mercy

mots.racine: ع ب د
mots.sens: His servant Zachariah

versets.ar: إِذْ نَادَىٰ رَبَّهُ نِدَاءً خَفِيًّا
versets.tr: idh nādā rabbahu nidāʾan khafiyyan
versets.trad: when he called upon his Lord with a secret call,
versets.commentaire: A key verse of the sūra's spirituality. Qushayrī: he hid his call from strangers so that none might perceive the secret of his state; indeed he would have hidden it from himself, closing his eyes to his own merits and holding himself in suspicion. He veiled his secret from creatures so that none might have sight of his state, and that his enemies might not gloat over his words. The secret call (nidāʾ khafiyy) is the seal of intimacy: the true devotee hides his prayer as one hides a treasure.

mots.racine: ن د و / ر ب ب
mots.sens: when he called his Lord

mots.racine: ن د و / خ ف ي
mots.sens: with a secret call

versets.ar: قَالَ رَبِّ إِنِّي وَهَنَ الْعَظْمُ مِنِّي وَاشْتَعَلَ الرَّأْسُ شَيْبًا وَلَمْ أَكُن بِدُعَائِكَ رَبِّ شَقِيًّا
versets.tr: qāla rabbi innī wahana l-ʿaẓmu minnī wa-shtaʿala r-raʾsu shayban wa-lam akun bi-duʿāʾika rabbi shaqiyyan
versets.trad: saying: “My Lord, my bones have grown frail and my head is alight with grey! Never, my Lord, have I been disappointed in praying to You!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: through my weakness in serving You I have met what I do not love: age has struck me, no strength remains after grey hair — so grant me a child to take my place in worshipping You. And on “never have I been wretched in calling You”: I ask You with the certainty of being answered, for I know I shall not be disappointed in my call — indeed, You love to be asked. He adds: You have accustomed me to answered prayer, and in past days never sent me away when I called.

mots.racine: و ه ن / ع ظ م
mots.sens: the bone has weakened in me

mots.racine: ش ع ل / ر أ س / ش ي ب
mots.sens: the head is ablaze with whiteness

mots.racine: د ع و / ش ق و
mots.sens: I have not been wretched in calling You

versets.ar: وَإِنِّي خِفْتُ الْمَوَالِيَ مِن وَرَائِي وَكَانَتِ امْرَأَتِي عَاقِرًا فَهَبْ لِي مِن لَّدُنكَ وَلِيًّا
versets.tr: wa-innī khiftu l-mawāliya min warāʾī wa-kānati mraʾatī ʿāqiran fa-hab lī min ladunka waliyyan
versets.trad: And I fear my kinsmen after me, for my wife is barren. So grant me from Yourself an heir,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: I feared the prophethood might leave my household and pass to my cousins; so grant me a child who will worship You and be of my line and my people. He notes that Zachariah did not desire the child out of craving for this world or to take its enjoyments, but that the child should fulfil the right of God. The plea for an heir is no carnal appetite but concern that the service of the Lord endure.

mots.racine: خ و ف / و ل ي
mots.sens: I feared my kinsmen after me

mots.racine: م ر أ / ع ق ر
mots.sens: my wife was barren

mots.racine: و ه ب / و ل ي
mots.sens: grant me from Yourself an heir
mots.def: wahaba: to bestow; waliyy: kin, successor.

versets.ar: يَرِثُنِي وَيَرِثُ مِنْ آلِ يَعْقُوبَ وَاجْعَلْهُ رَبِّ رَضِيًّا
versets.tr: yarithunī wa-yarithu min āli yaʿqūba wa-jʿalhu rabbi raḍiyyan
versets.trad: who will inherit from me and inherit from the house of Jacob. And make him, my Lord, pleasing!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sees in “he will inherit from me” the plea for a survival: a child who remains after me, who inherits from the house of Jacob the prophethood and the conveying of the message. Of “pleasing” (raḍiyy) he gives two senses: one whom You approve, so that he be approved by You; or, by intensity, one himself content with You and pleased with what You decree. The coveted inheritance is no possession, but a holy charge.

mots.racine: و ر ث
mots.sens: he inherits from me and from Jacob's house
mots.def: waritha: to inherit; āl: house, family.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / ر ض و
mots.sens: make him, Lord, pleasing

versets.ar: يَا زَكَرِيَّا إِنَّا نُبَشِّرُكَ بِغُلَامٍ اسْمُهُ يَحْيَىٰ لَمْ نَجْعَل لَّهُ مِن قَبْلُ سَمِيًّا
versets.tr: yā zakariyyā innā nubashshiruka bi-ghulāmin smuhu yaḥyā lam najʿal lahu min qablu samiyyan
versets.trad: “O Zachariah! We give you glad tidings of a boy whose name shall be John (Yaḥyā), a name We have given to none before him.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: We have answered your prayer and grant you a son named Yaḥyā — through him the barrenness of his mother will live again (yaḥyā), your line will live, your remembrance will live, and in your house the place of worship and prophethood will live. On “We gave him no namesake before”: his uniqueness in name signals his uniqueness in merit; none equalled him in the sum of his virtues.

mots.racine: ب ش ر / غ ل م
mots.sens: We give you tidings of a boy
mots.def: bashshara: to give glad tidings; ghulām: boy.

mots.racine: س م و / ح ي ي
mots.sens: his name is Yaḥyā

mots.ar: لَمْ نَجْعَل لَّهُ مِن قَبْلُ سَمِيًّا
mots.tr: lam najʿal lahu min qablu samiyyan
mots.racine: ج ع ل / س م و
mots.sens: We gave him no namesake before

versets.ar: قَالَ رَبِّ أَنَّىٰ يَكُونُ لِي غُلَامٌ وَكَانَتِ امْرَأَتِي عَاقِرًا وَقَدْ بَلَغْتُ مِنَ الْكِبَرِ عِتِيًّا
versets.tr: qāla rabbi annā yakūnu lī ghulāmun wa-kānati mraʾatī ʿāqiran wa-qad balaghtu mina l-kibari ʿitiyyan
versets.trad: He said: “My Lord! How shall I have a boy when my wife is barren and I have reached the extremity of old age?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī explains the wonder of one who had himself asked: according to the exegetes, a long interval separated his request from its answer; he had sought the child at the beginning of his age and was answered when he had reached extreme old age — hence his “how?”. Another reading: he wished to know from whom the child would come — this barren wife, or another he might marry? The question is no doubt, but a desire to grasp the mode of the wonder.

mots.racine: غ ل م
mots.sens: how shall I have a boy?

mots.racine: ب ل غ / ك ب ر / ع ت و
mots.sens: I have reached extreme old age
mots.def: ʿitiyy: decrepitude, the dryness of age.

versets.ar: قَالَ كَذَٰلِكَ قَالَ رَبُّكَ هُوَ عَلَيَّ هَيِّنٌ وَقَدْ خَلَقْتُكَ مِن قَبْلُ وَلَمْ تَكُ شَيْئًا
versets.tr: qāla kadhālika qāla rabbuka huwa ʿalayya hayyinun wa-qad khalaqtuka min qablu wa-lam taku shayʾan
versets.trad: He said: “So it shall be! Your Lord has said: ‘That is easy for Me. I created you before, when you were nothing.’”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the answering by a child, at a time when custom holds that such a woman does not bear, is for you a wonder and a sign before your people: the child will be for you, on one side, a miracle, and on another, repose and honour. On “I created you when you were nothing”: the verse proves that pure non-being is not a thing, for God denies that before creating him he was anything at all.

mots.racine: ه و ن
mots.sens: that is easy for Me

mots.ar: وَقَدْ خَلَقْتُكَ مِن قَبْلُ وَلَمْ تَكُ شَيْئًا
mots.tr: wa-qad khalaqtuka min qablu wa-lam taku shayʾan
mots.racine: خ ل ق / ش ي أ
mots.sens: I created you when you were nothing

versets.ar: قَالَ رَبِّ اجْعَل لِّي آيَةً قَالَ آيَتُكَ أَلَّا تُكَلِّمَ النَّاسَ ثَلَاثَ لَيَالٍ سَوِيًّا
versets.tr: qāla rabbi jʿal lī āyatan qāla āyatuka allā tukallima n-nāsa thalātha layālin sawiyyan
versets.trad: He said: “My Lord, appoint for me a sign!” He said: “Your sign is that you shall not speak to people for three full nights.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he wished a mark of the child's conception, not a proof of the tidings' truthfulness. God gives him for a sign a selective silence: your tongue, despite every effort, will not address people for three days; but you shall speak to Me and recite the revealed Scriptures. His tongue would freeze when he wished to speak to people, and loosen the moment he wished to read or glorify God. Muteness before creatures, eloquence before the Real: image of the saint detached from the world.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / أ ي ي
mots.sens: Lord, appoint for me a sign

mots.racine: ك ل م / ل ي ل
mots.sens: that you not speak to people three nights

versets.ar: فَخَرَجَ عَلَىٰ قَوْمِهِ مِنَ الْمِحْرَابِ فَأَوْحَىٰ إِلَيْهِمْ أَن سَبِّحُوا بُكْرَةً وَعَشِيًّا
versets.tr: fa-kharaja ʿalā qawmihi mina l-miḥrābi fa-awḥā ilayhim an sabbiḥū bukratan wa-ʿashiyyan
versets.trad: So he came forth to his people from the sanctuary and signalled to them: Glorify [God] morning and evening.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when he appeared before them, he made known to them — by gesture alone, by allusion — that the tongue with which he used to address them was now no longer loosed. Deprived of speech, Zachariah communicates by signs (ishāra): and it is this wordless communication that signifies to them to glorify God at dawn and at dusk. The language of silence supplies for speech, and the allusion carries what the word can no longer say.

mots.racine: خ ر ج / ح ر ب
mots.sens: he came forth from the sanctuary to his people

mots.racine: س ب ح / ب ك ر / ع ش و
mots.sens: glorify morning and evening
mots.def: sabbaḥa: to glorify; bukra: morning; ʿashiyy: evening.

versets.ar: يَا يَحْيَىٰ خُذِ الْكِتَابَ بِقُوَّةٍ وَآتَيْنَاهُ الْحُكْمَ صَبِيًّا
versets.tr: yā yaḥyā khudhi l-kitāba bi-quwwatin wa-ātaynāhu l-ḥukma ṣabiyyan
versets.trad: “O John, hold fast to the Scripture!” And We gave him wisdom while yet a child,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: We said to him: O Yaḥyā, take the Book with a strength We have bestowed on you — not the strength of the hand, but the strength of the heart; and that good with which God singled him out is prophethood. On “We gave him wisdom while a child”: the ḥukm is prophethood: God sent him to his people and revealed to him while he was small; and it is said: it is just and true judgment among men, or the firmness of action in the mode of command. Grace outruns effort: wisdom was given, not acquired.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / ق و ي
mots.sens: take the Book with strength

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ح ك م / ص ب و
mots.sens: We gave him wisdom as a child
mots.def: ḥukm: wisdom, judgment, prophethood; ṣabiyy: child.

versets.ar: وَحَنَانًا مِّن لَّدُنَّا وَزَكَاةً وَكَانَ تَقِيًّا
versets.tr: wa-ḥanānan min ladunnā wa-zakātan wa-kāna taqiyyan
versets.trad: and tenderness from Us, and purity; and he was God-fearing,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “and a tenderness from Us”: We gave him a mercy from Our presence, a purity, and the grace to realize the fruits of piety (taqwā). He then distinguishes two pieties: an acquired piety, at which the servant arrives by his effort and learning; and a piety deposited by God, which He makes feared, and which the servant receives by the gift and favour of the Most High. John's ḥanān is of this second order: not conquered, but infused.

mots.racine: ح ن ن
mots.sens: tenderness from Us

mots.racine: ز ك و / و ق ي
mots.sens: purity; he was God-fearing

versets.ar: وَبَرًّا بِوَالِدَيْهِ وَلَمْ يَكُن جَبَّارًا عَصِيًّا
versets.tr: wa-barran bi-wālidayhi wa-lam yakun jabbāran ʿaṣiyyan
versets.trad: and dutiful to his parents; and he was neither arrogant nor rebellious.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: “dutiful to his parents”: as a command of God — not from mere human affection nor from the obligation of nature. John's filial devotion is no instinct but obedience: he honours father and mother because God commands it. And “he was neither tyrant nor rebel”: he did not revolt against the Truth nor deny His lordship.

mots.racine: ب ر ر / و ل د
mots.sens: dutiful to his parents
mots.def: barr: dutiful, kind; wālidān: the two parents.

mots.racine: ج ب ر / ع ص ي
mots.sens: he was neither tyrant nor rebel

versets.ar: وَسَلَامٌ عَلَيْهِ يَوْمَ وُلِدَ وَيَوْمَ يَمُوتُ وَيَوْمَ يُبْعَثُ حَيًّا
versets.tr: wa-salāmun ʿalayhi yawma wulida wa-yawma yamūtu wa-yawma yubʿathu ḥayyan
versets.trad: Peace be upon him the day he was born, the day he dies, and the day he is raised alive!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he has from Us a safekeeping on the Day of resurrection, on the day of his birth at the beginning, and on the day of his death at the end — God preserves him from deviation and crookedness in belief, by what he ceaselessly contemplates of the divine reality. Thus: in this world he is preserved from fault, guarded from harm; and in the other, preserved from trial and affliction. The peace that wraps the three thresholds — to be born, to die, to be raised — is the mark of the elect.

mots.racine: س ل م / و ل د
mots.sens: peace upon him the day he was born

mots.racine: ب ع ث / ح ي ي
mots.sens: the day he is raised alive
mots.def: buʿitha: to be raised; ḥayy: alive.

versets.ar: وَاذْكُرْ فِي الْكِتَابِ مَرْيَمَ إِذِ انتَبَذَتْ مِنْ أَهْلِهَا مَكَانًا شَرْقِيًّا
versets.tr: wa-dhkur fī l-kitābi maryama idhi ntabadhat min ahlihā makānan sharqiyyan
versets.trad: And mention in the Book Mary, when she withdrew from her family to a place toward the east.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: she drew apart from them seeking a purification that would make her pure, and hid herself from their gaze. Mary's withdrawal (intibādh) toward the east is a gesture of recollection: she separates from her kin to stand alone before God. The eastern place, whence light rises, figures the heart's orientation toward the source of day.

mots.racine: ن ب ذ / أ ه ل
mots.sens: when she withdrew from her family

mots.racine: ك و ن / ش ر ق
mots.sens: to an eastern place

versets.ar: فَاتَّخَذَتْ مِن دُونِهِمْ حِجَابًا فَأَرْسَلْنَا إِلَيْهَا رُوحَنَا فَتَمَثَّلَ لَهَا بَشَرًا سَوِيًّا
versets.tr: fa-ttakhadhat min dūnihim ḥijāban fa-arsalnā ilayhā rūḥanā fa-tamaththala lahā basharan sawiyyan
versets.trad: She took a veil apart from them. Then We sent to her Our Spirit, and he appeared to her as a perfect man.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: she set herself apart from them to attain a purity, and veiled herself from their eyes. When she saw Gabriel in a human form she did not expect, she felt terror within her, and had no recourse but to frighten him with the name of God and to turn back to God. The appearing of the Spirit (rūḥ) as a perfect man (bashar sawiyy) is the threshold of the wonder: Mary, alone and veiled, opposes to the unknown only her refuge in God.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / ح ج ب
mots.sens: she took a veil apart from them

mots.racine: ر س ل / ر و ح
mots.sens: We sent to her Our Spirit

mots.racine: م ث ل / ب ش ر / س و ي
mots.sens: he appeared to her as a perfect man
mots.def: tamaththala: to take shape; bashar sawiyy: a perfect man.

versets.ar: قَالَتْ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِالرَّحْمَٰنِ مِنكَ إِن كُنتَ تَقِيًّا
versets.tr: qālat innī aʿūdhu bi-r-raḥmāni minka in kunta taqiyyan
versets.trad: She said: “I take refuge in the All-Merciful from you, if you fear God!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Mary said to Gabriel, whom she did not recognize: I take refuge in the All-Merciful from you, if you are of those to be feared — that is, if you intend harm. And he dwells on the choice of the Name: she said “the All-Merciful” and not “God”: by the One who shows me mercy and guards me from you. In peril, the pure soul calls upon the attribute of gentleness, not of rigour: it is raḥma she invokes as her shield.

mots.racine: ع و ذ / ر ح م
mots.sens: I take refuge in the All-Merciful from you
mots.def: ʿādha: to seek refuge; al-Raḥmān: the All-Merciful.

mots.racine: و ق ي
mots.sens: if you fear God

versets.ar: قَالَ إِنَّمَا أَنَا رَسُولُ رَبِّكِ لِأَهَبَ لَكِ غُلَامًا زَكِيًّا
versets.tr: qāla innamā anā rasūlu rabbiki li-ahaba laki ghulāman zakiyyan
versets.trad: He said: “I am only the messenger of your Lord, to grant you a pure boy.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Gabriel made himself known to her by what stilled her terror, and joined his words to the glad tidings of Jesus. The angel dispels her fear by revealing his mission: he is but a messenger, and his message is a gift — a pure son (zakiyy). Calming precedes annunciation: God first quiets the heart before depositing in it the secret.

mots.racine: ر س ل / ر ب ب
mots.sens: I am only the messenger of your Lord

mots.racine: و ه ب / ز ك و
mots.sens: to grant you a pure boy

versets.ar: قَالَتْ أَنَّىٰ يَكُونُ لِي غُلَامٌ وَلَمْ يَمْسَسْنِي بَشَرٌ وَلَمْ أَكُ بَغِيًّا
versets.tr: qālat annā yakūnu lī ghulāmun wa-lam yamsasnī basharun wa-lam aku baghiyyan
versets.trad: She said: “How shall I have a boy when no mortal has ever touched me, and I am no harlot?”
versets.commentaire: Mary's wonder before the inconceivable. Qushayrī: how shall I have a child, I who have known neither fault nor lewdness? She does not doubt the divine power, but questions the mode: no man has approached her, no misconduct stains her. The very question attests her purity: she forestalls every suspicion by proclaiming her chastity.

mots.racine: م س س / ب ش ر
mots.sens: no mortal has touched me
mots.def: massa: to touch; bashar: human being.

mots.racine: ب غ ي
mots.sens: I am no harlot
mots.def: baghiyy: a woman of ill repute.

versets.ar: قَالَ كَذَٰلِكِ قَالَ رَبُّكِ هُوَ عَلَيَّ هَيِّنٌ وَلِنَجْعَلَهُ آيَةً لِّلنَّاسِ وَرَحْمَةً مِّنَّا وَكَانَ أَمْرًا مَّقْضِيًّا
versets.tr: qāla kadhāliki qāla rabbuki huwa ʿalayya hayyinun wa-li-najʿalahu āyatan li-n-nāsi wa-raḥmatan minnā wa-kāna amran maqḍiyyan
versets.trad: He said: “So it is; your Lord has said: ‘That is easy for Me. And [it is] that We may make him a sign for mankind and a mercy from Us. It is a matter decreed.’”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: Gabriel answered: the matter is as you said; nothing of this resists God, for He is more than able to make this child a proof of the perfection of His power. This child will be a mercy from Him for whoever believes, and a cause of blindness for others. The same wonder is light and trial: a sign for mankind, it parts those who bow from those who close themselves. And this decree is “fulfilled”: irrevocable.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / أ ي ي
mots.sens: to make him a sign for mankind

mots.racine: أ م ر / ق ض ي
mots.sens: it is a matter decreed

versets.ar: فَحَمَلَتْهُ فَانتَبَذَتْ بِهِ مَكَانًا قَصِيًّا
versets.tr: fa-ḥamalathu fa-ntabadhat bihi makānan qaṣiyyan
versets.trad: So she conceived him and withdrew with him to a far place.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when the pregnancy showed upon her, and she knew that people would deem it improbable, having no confidant to whom to disclose her secret, she went off to a place far from people. Mary's solitude is no flight but necessity: bearing a secret none would believe, she withdraws where no eye seeks her. The heart's secret finds refuge in remoteness, when men could not receive it.

mots.racine: ح م ل
mots.sens: she conceived him

mots.racine: ن ب ذ / ق ص و
mots.sens: she withdrew with him to a far place

versets.ar: فَأَجَاءَهَا الْمَخَاضُ إِلَىٰ جِذْعِ النَّخْلَةِ قَالَتْ يَا لَيْتَنِي مِتُّ قَبْلَ هَٰذَا وَكُنتُ نَسْيًا مَّنسِيًّا
versets.tr: fa-ajāʾahā l-makhāḍu ilā jidhʿi n-nakhlati qālat yā laytanī mittu qabla hādhā wa-kuntu nasyan mansiyyan
versets.trad: The pangs of childbirth drove her to the trunk of the palm tree. She said: “Would that I had died before this, and were a thing forgotten, utterly forgotten!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the pain of labour forced her to lean upon the trunk of the palm. Seized by the contractions and by shame before her people, she spoke the language of helplessness: ‘would that I had died before this!’ He offers several readings of this cry: perhaps out of fear of her kin, knowing they would unleash against her the tongue of blame; or out of compassion for them, lest a punishment befall them on her account; or that she might not have to hear it said of God, through her, that Jesus is the son of God — God is far above that! And this admirable word: ‘would that I had died’ at the time when my heart was bound to no cause.

mots.racine: ج ي أ / م خ ض / ج ذ ع / ن خ ل
mots.sens: the pangs drove her to the palm trunk
mots.def: makhāḍ: pangs of childbirth; jidhʿ: trunk.

mots.racine: م و ت
mots.sens: would that I had died before this
mots.def: layta: would that; māta: to die.

mots.racine: ن س ي
mots.sens: and were forgotten, utterly forgotten
mots.def: nasy mansiyy: a thing wholly forgotten.

versets.ar: فَنَادَاهَا مِن تَحْتِهَا أَلَّا تَحْزَنِي قَدْ جَعَلَ رَبُّكِ تَحْتَكِ سَرِيًّا
versets.tr: fa-nādāhā min taḥtihā allā taḥzanī qad jaʿala rabbuki taḥtaki sariyyan
versets.trad: A voice called to her from below her: “Do not grieve! Your Lord has placed beneath you a rivulet.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the exegetes differ over ‘from below her’: for some it is Gabriel, for others Jesus himself. And the meaning: to still the terror that was in her, and to announce Jesus to her — your Lord has bestowed on you a noble child (sariyy). At the depth of distress, the voice comes “from below”: consolation rises from the very place of abasement. When none watches over her, God Himself undertakes to suffice her.

mots.racine: ن د و / ت ح ت
mots.sens: he called her from below her

mots.racine: ج ع ل / س ر ي
mots.sens: your Lord has set beneath you a rivulet
mots.def: sariyy: a rivulet; or a noble man.

versets.ar: وَهُزِّي إِلَيْكِ بِجِذْعِ النَّخْلَةِ تُسَاقِطْ عَلَيْكِ رُطَبًا جَنِيًّا
versets.tr: wa-huzzī ilayki bi-jidhʿi n-nakhlati tusāqiṭ ʿalayki ruṭaban janiyyan
versets.trad: Shake toward you the trunk of the palm; it will let fall upon you fresh ripe dates.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: it was a withered trunk, from which God brought forth fruit on the spot — the fresh ripe date; there was in this for her a sign and a proof: He who can do such a thing can create Jesus without a father. He adds a spiritual subtlety: when she was wholly stripped, with no attachment, Zachariah used to find provision with her without her having to do anything; but when the attachment of the child came, she was commanded to shake the dry palm — at the weakest of her states — that it be known that attachment imposes toil and hardship. And again: having then none to tend her, God undertook to suffice her Himself, that the world might know He does not abandon the elite of His servants in their time of need.

mots.racine: ه ز ز / ج ذ ع / ن خ ل
mots.sens: shake toward you the palm trunk

mots.racine: س ق ط / ر ط ب / ج ن ي
mots.sens: it will drop fresh ripe dates
mots.def: ruṭab: fresh dates; janiyy: newly gathered.

versets.ar: فَكُلِي وَاشْرَبِي وَقَرِّي عَيْنًا فَإِمَّا تَرَيِنَّ مِنَ الْبَشَرِ أَحَدًا فَقُولِي إِنِّي نَذَرْتُ لِلرَّحْمَٰنِ صَوْمًا فَلَنْ أُكَلِّمَ الْيَوْمَ إِنسِيًّا
versets.tr: fa-kulī wa-shrabī wa-qarrī ʿaynan fa-immā tarayinna mina l-bashari aḥadan fa-qūlī innī nadhartu li-r-raḥmāni ṣawman fa-lan ukallima l-yawma insiyyan
versets.trad: Eat, drink, and be comforted. And if you see any mortal, say: ‘I have vowed a fast to the All-Merciful; today I will speak to no one.’”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He sufficed her in all she needed of eating and drinking, stilled her dread, and gladdened her heart. Then on the vow of silence: ‘if you see any human,’ do not speak to him, but make known to him by a sign that you have vowed to the All-Merciful silence with creatures and abstention from all converse. The highest fast is not of food but of speech: to be silent before men so as to converse with God alone.

mots.racine: أ ك ل / ش ر ب / ق ر ر / ع ي ن
mots.sens: eat, drink and rejoice
mots.def: qarrat ʿaynuhā: to have the eye stilled, to rejoice.

mots.racine: ن ذ ر / ص و م
mots.sens: I have vowed a fast to the All-Merciful
mots.def: nadhara: to vow; ṣawm: fast (here of silence).

mots.racine: ك ل م / أ ن س
mots.sens: I will speak to no human today

versets.ar: فَأَتَتْ بِهِ قَوْمَهَا تَحْمِلُهُ قَالُوا يَا مَرْيَمُ لَقَدْ جِئْتِ شَيْئًا فَرِيًّا
versets.tr: fa-atat bihi qawmahā taḥmiluhu qālū yā maryamu laqad jiʾti shayʾan fariyyan
versets.trad: Then she brought him to her people, carrying him. They said: “O Mary! You have done a monstrous thing!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: her people unleashed against her the tongue of blame when they saw she had borne a child — the outward appearance was against her — and they said to her, by way of reproach: O you whom we counted among the virtuous, whence comes this monstrous deed? The “monstrous thing” (shayʾ fariyy) is the verdict of eyes that see only the exterior. Mary, bearer of the wonder, has for answer only the promised silence.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ح م ل
mots.sens: she came with him to her people, carrying him
mots.def: atā bi-: to bring; ḥamala: to carry.

mots.racine: ج ي أ / ف ر ي
mots.sens: you have committed a monstrous thing

versets.ar: يَا أُخْتَ هَارُونَ مَا كَانَ أَبُوكِ امْرَأَ سَوْءٍ وَمَا كَانَتْ أُمُّكِ بَغِيًّا
versets.tr: yā ukhta hārūna mā kāna abūki mraʾa sawʾin wa-mā kānat ummuki baghiyyan
versets.trad: O sister of Aaron! Your father was no evil man, nor was your mother a harlot.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī reports the readings of “sister of Aaron”: O you whom we held, in virtue, to be like Aaron, known for his rectitude and piety — whence comes this foulness to you? Or: she had a brother named Aaron; or Aaron was a profligate of their people, and they said: O you like him in corruption; or Aaron was a virtuous man among them: O sister of Aaron, you whose parents, by our reckoning, had neither evil nor corruption — how did you commit this enormity? The mention of pious parents sharpens the reproach: the purity of the line makes the apparent fault more glaring.

mots.racine: أ خ و
mots.sens: O sister of Aaron
mots.def: ukht: sister; a reminder of the line of ʿImrān.

mots.racine: أ ب و / س و أ
mots.sens: your father was no evil man

versets.ar: فَأَشَارَتْ إِلَيْهِ قَالُوا كَيْفَ نُكَلِّمُ مَن كَانَ فِي الْمَهْدِ صَبِيًّا
versets.tr: fa-ashārat ilayhi qālū kayfa nukallimu man kāna fī l-mahdi ṣabiyyan
versets.trad: She pointed to him. They said: “How can we speak to one who is a child in the cradle?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī gives here one of his finest penetrations: outwardly she pointed to the child, but in secret she pointed to God. They are indignant: how shall we speak to one who deserves still to be laid in the cradle? — taking the thing for mockery. But Mary's gesture is twofold: the finger points to the infant, the heart points to the One who will make the infant speak. The referral to the child is in truth a referral to God, sole author of the imminent wonder.

mots.racine: ش و ر
mots.sens: she pointed to him

mots.ar: كَيْفَ نُكَلِّمُ مَن كَانَ فِي الْمَهْدِ صَبِيًّا
mots.tr: kayfa nukallimu man kāna fī l-mahdi ṣabiyyan
mots.racine: ك ل م / م ه د / ص ب و
mots.sens: how speak to a child in the cradle?

versets.ar: قَالَ إِنِّي عَبْدُ اللَّهِ آتَانِيَ الْكِتَابَ وَجَعَلَنِي نَبِيًّا
versets.tr: qāla innī ʿabdu llāhi ātāniya l-kitāba wa-jaʿalanī nabiyyan
versets.trad: He said: “I am, in truth, the servant of God. He has given me the Scripture and made me a prophet,
versets.commentaire: The summit of the narrative. Qushayrī: when they said this, God made Jesus speak, and he said: ‘I am the servant of God’ — and Mary's innocence burst forth through the speech of Jesus before he could speak as a man. He draws from it an argument against divinization: if Jesus is truthful in saying he is the servant of God, your doctrine of the trinity collapses; and if he lies, the liar is no son of God but a servant of God. Then this sublime definition of servitude: he who is the slave of no passion and captive of none but Him, he who has freed himself from all but Him, is in truth His servant.

mots.racine: ع ب د
mots.sens: I am the servant of God

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ك ت ب / ج ع ل / ن ب أ
mots.sens: He gave me the Book and made me a prophet

versets.ar: وَجَعَلَنِي مُبَارَكًا أَيْنَ مَا كُنتُ وَأَوْصَانِي بِالصَّلَاةِ وَالزَّكَاةِ مَا دُمْتُ حَيًّا
versets.tr: wa-jaʿalanī mubārakan ayna mā kuntu wa-awṣānī bi-ṣ-ṣalāti wa-z-zakāti mā dumtu ḥayyan
versets.trad: and He has made me blessed wherever I am, and enjoined upon me prayer and almsgiving as long as I live,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī unfolds the blessing (baraka) of Jesus: useful to creatures, guiding them in the affairs of their religion, turning them from the fault wherein lies their ruin; whoever takes light from his light is saved. He enumerates his benefits: to succour the afflicted, aid the weak, defend the wronged, relieve the poor, guide the strayed, counsel mankind, ward off harm from them and bear the harm they do. Sanctity is no withdrawal but service: to be “blessed wherever I am” is to carry grace wherever one passes.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / ب ر ك / ك و ن
mots.sens: He made me blessed wherever I am

mots.racine: و ص ي / ص ل و / ز ك و
mots.sens: He enjoined on me prayer and almsgiving

versets.ar: وَبَرًّا بِوَالِدَتِي وَلَمْ يَجْعَلْنِي جَبَّارًا شَقِيًّا
versets.tr: wa-barran bi-wālidatī wa-lam yajʿalnī jabbāran shaqiyyan
versets.trad: and dutiful to my mother. He has not made me arrogant or wretched.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: ‘dutiful to my mother’; and ‘He has not made me a wretched tyrant’: that is, He has not made me rebellious to counsel, incapable of receiving it. He glosses “shaqiyy”: arrogant and despotic; or sealed with unbelief. Felicity (saʿāda) lies in the humility that can receive; wretchedness (shaqāwa), in the hardness that closes itself to all admonition.

mots.racine: ب ر ر / و ل د
mots.sens: dutiful to my mother

mots.racine: ج ب ر / ش ق و
mots.sens: He made me no wretched tyrant

versets.ar: وَالسَّلَامُ عَلَيَّ يَوْمَ وُلِدتُّ وَيَوْمَ أَمُوتُ وَيَوْمَ أُبْعَثُ حَيًّا
versets.tr: wa-s-salāmu ʿalayya yawma wulidtu wa-yawma amūtu wa-yawma ubʿathu ḥayyan
versets.trad: And peace be upon me the day I was born, the day I die, and the day I am raised alive.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī finely contrasts the “salām” of Jesus with the greeting given to the Prophet: Jesus said ‘peace upon me,’ while to our Prophet, on the night of the ascension, it was said: ‘peace upon you, O Prophet’ — what a distance between the two! And the sense of the peace: safekeeping the day of my birth against the excess of the Christians in praise and against the blame of the Jews; I am neither what the one said nor what the other said. Peace frames the three thresholds — to be born, to die, to be raised — as a veil of preservation against the two opposite errors.

mots.racine: س ل م / و ل د
mots.sens: peace upon me the day I was born

mots.racine: ب ع ث / ح ي ي
mots.sens: the day I am raised alive
mots.def: buʿitha: to be raised; ḥayy: alive.

versets.ar: ذَٰلِكَ عِيسَى ابْنُ مَرْيَمَ قَوْلَ الْحَقِّ الَّذِي فِيهِ يَمْتَرُونَ
versets.tr: dhālika ʿīsā bnu maryama qawla l-ḥaqqi lladhī fīhi yamtarūna
versets.trad: Such is Jesus, son of Mary — the word of truth, about which they are in doubt.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he who said what God reports of him is Jesus, son of Mary — could this be the word of a god? And on the doubt: most of mankind doubted him: one party rejected him, one party accepted him; the difference between them lies in their share of the Truth. Jesus is “the word of truth” (qawl al-ḥaqq): a Word issued from the divine “Be!” The dividing of men over him reveals their own relation to the Real.

mots.sens: such is Jesus son of Mary

mots.racine: ق و ل / ح ق ق / م ر ي
mots.sens: the word of truth about which they doubt

versets.ar: مَا كَانَ لِلَّهِ أَن يَتَّخِذَ مِن وَلَدٍ سُبْحَانَهُ إِذَا قَضَىٰ أَمْرًا فَإِنَّمَا يَقُولُ لَهُ كُن فَيَكُونُ
versets.tr: mā kāna li-llāhi an yattakhidha min waladin subḥānahu idhā qaḍā amran fa-innamā yaqūlu lahu kun fa-yakūnu
versets.trad: It is not for God to take a child. Glory be to Him! When He decrees a thing, He but says to it: ‘Be!’ and it is.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: it cannot be that He has a child in truth, for He is the One, and the child is a part of its begetter. God has no need of a consort from whom a son might come, nor can He adopt, for lack of any like Him. On “Be, and it is”: when He wills to bring forth a thing, He creates it by His power and addresses to it the command of existence; nothing possible, in truth, resists Him. The “kun” is the pure act: God does not beget, He makes be.

mots.ar: مَا كَانَ لِلَّهِ أَن يَتَّخِذَ مِن وَلَدٍ
mots.tr: mā kāna li-llāhi an yattakhidha min waladin
mots.racine: أ خ ذ / و ل د
mots.sens: it is not for God to take a child

mots.racine: ق و ل / ك و ن
mots.sens: He says to it ‘Be’ and it is

versets.ar: وَإِنَّ اللَّهَ رَبِّي وَرَبُّكُمْ فَاعْبُدُوهُ هَٰذَا صِرَاطٌ مُّسْتَقِيمٌ
versets.tr: wa-inna llāha rabbī wa-rabbukum fa-ʿbudūhu hādhā ṣirāṭun mustaqīmun
versets.trad: [Jesus said:] “God is, in truth, my Lord and your Lord. So worship Him! This is the straight path!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: ‘God is my Lord and your Lord’: He commanded me to make this known to you, ordered me to convey my message and to follow the acts of worship He has prescribed. Jesus himself proclaims the common servitude: no divinized intermediary, but a single Lordship for the Messiah as for mankind. The straight path (ṣirāṭ mustaqīm) is this acknowledgment: to worship the One, associating nothing with Him.

mots.racine: ر ب ب
mots.sens: God is my Lord and yours

mots.racine: ع ب د / ص ر ط / ق و م
mots.sens: worship Him, this is the straight path

versets.ar: فَاخْتَلَفَ الْأَحْزَابُ مِن بَيْنِهِمْ فَوَيْلٌ لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِن مَّشْهَدِ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ
versets.tr: fa-khtalafa l-aḥzābu min baynihim fa-waylun lilladhīna kafarū min mashhadi yawmin ʿaẓīmin
versets.trad: But the factions differed among themselves. Woe to those who disbelieved at the appearing of a tremendous Day!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī resolves it by predestination: he whose clay was kneaded with the water of felicity obeys in this world and is not lost in the next; he whom the ancient apportioning kept at a distance, later service does not bring near — and they will taste the sequel of this affair. The factions (aḥzāb) that divided over Jesus illustrate this primal parting: the divergence of doctrines reflects the diversity of destinies, and the tremendous Day will reveal its outcome.

mots.racine: خ ل ف / ح ز ب
mots.sens: the factions differed among themselves

mots.ar: فَوَيْلٌ لِّلَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِن مَّشْهَدِ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ
mots.tr: fa-waylun lilladhīna kafarū min mashhadi yawmin ʿaẓīmin
mots.racine: و ي ل / ك ف ر / ش ه د
mots.sens: woe to the disbelievers at the great Day's appearing

versets.ar: أَسْمِعْ بِهِمْ وَأَبْصِرْ يَوْمَ يَأْتُونَنَا لَٰكِنِ الظَّالِمُونَ الْيَوْمَ فِي ضَلَالٍ مُّبِينٍ
versets.tr: asmiʿ bihim wa-abṣir yawma yaʾtūnanā lākini ẓ-ẓālimūna l-yawma fī ḍalālin mubīnin
versets.trad: How clearly they will hear and see the Day they come to Us! But today the wrongdoers are in manifest error.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī paints the reversal of that Day: their knowledge will become compelled, all their states overturned; the proof will be fixed against them, their need no longer heard, mercy no longer attached to them; their complaint will not be pitied, their call not heeded. On that Day their hearing and sight will at last be keen — too late. The blindness chosen here below becomes a forced clear-sight there, but one that no longer avails.

mots.racine: س م ع / ب ص ر
mots.sens: how they will hear and see!

mots.racine: ظ ل م / ض ل ل / ب ي ن
mots.sens: the wrongdoers are in manifest error

versets.ar: وَأَنذِرْهُمْ يَوْمَ الْحَسْرَةِ إِذْ قُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ وَهُمْ فِي غَفْلَةٍ وَهُمْ لَا يُؤْمِنُونَ
versets.tr: wa-andhirhum yawma l-ḥasrati idh quḍiya l-amru wa-hum fī ghaflatin wa-hum lā yuʾminūna
versets.trad: And warn them of the Day of Regret, when the matter is decided, while they are in heedlessness and do not believe.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Hour will come upon them unawares, the resurrection will surprise them unprepared, and they will lament over what has escaped them. He gives too a mystical reading: the Day of Regret is the day of the apportioning, when wretchedness was decreed for some — still effaced in non-being — and felicity for others — still inexistent; no crime had yet come from the first, no concord from the second. The supreme regret (ḥasra) is born of seeing, too late, the decree already sealed.

mots.racine: ن ذ ر / ح س ر
mots.sens: warn them of the Day of Regret

mots.ar: إِذْ قُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ وَهُمْ فِي غَفْلَةٍ
mots.tr: idh quḍiya l-amru wa-hum fī ghaflatin
mots.racine: ق ض ي / أ م ر / غ ف ل
mots.sens: when the matter is decided, they heedless
mots.def: quḍiya: to be decided; ghafla: heedlessness.

versets.ar: إِنَّا نَحْنُ نَرِثُ الْأَرْضَ وَمَنْ عَلَيْهَا وَإِلَيْنَا يُرْجَعُونَ
versets.tr: innā naḥnu narithu l-arḍa wa-man ʿalayhā wa-ilaynā yurjaʿūna
versets.trad: It is We who shall inherit the earth and all who are on it, and to Us they shall be returned.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī links this verse to the pleas for inheritance in the sūra: Zachariah said ‘he will inherit from me and from the house of Jacob’; God said of the children of Israel ‘thus We made the children of Israel inherit it’; and reaching this community, He said: ‘It is We who shall inherit the earth.’ Then this admirable point: what a distance between one whose heir is his son and one whose heir is the One! It is light for the believer to die when the Real is his heir. For God to inherit is not to acquire, but to remain alone when all passes away.

mots.ar: إِنَّا نَحْنُ نَرِثُ الْأَرْضَ وَمَنْ عَلَيْهَا
mots.tr: innā naḥnu narithu l-arḍa wa-man ʿalayhā
mots.racine: و ر ث / أ ر ض
mots.sens: We inherit the earth and those on it
mots.def: waritha: to inherit (to remain alone after).

mots.racine: ر ج ع
mots.sens: to Us they are returned

versets.ar: وَاذْكُرْ فِي الْكِتَابِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ إِنَّهُ كَانَ صِدِّيقًا نَّبِيًّا
versets.tr: wa-dhkur fī l-kitābi ibrāhīma innahu kāna ṣiddīqan nabiyyan
versets.trad: And mention in the Book Abraham. He was a man of truth, a prophet.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī multiplies the definitions of the “truthful one” (ṣiddīq): he whose sincerity is mixed with no alloy; the true in his words, his acts, and his states; he whose secret does not contradict his open self; he who attests, in affirming or denying, none but God; he who responds to all that is asked of him, in sum and in detail; he who stands with God, at all times, on the limit of truth. The ṣiddīq is the man all of one piece: inner and outer attuned to the Real.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / ك ت ب
mots.sens: mention Abraham in the Book

mots.racine: ص د ق / ن ب أ
mots.sens: he was truthful, a prophet

versets.ar: إِذْ قَالَ لِأَبِيهِ يَا أَبَتِ لِمَ تَعْبُدُ مَا لَا يَسْمَعُ وَلَا يُبْصِرُ وَلَا يُغْنِي عَنكَ شَيْئًا
versets.tr: idh qāla li-abīhi yā abati lima taʿbudu mā lā yasmaʿu wa-lā yubṣiru wa-lā yughnī ʿanka shayʾan
versets.trad: When he said to his father: “O my father! Why do you worship what neither hears nor sees nor avails you anything?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the verse proves that the object of worship rightly merits being described by hearing and sight in their perfection, without defect, and likewise the power to harm and to profit. Then he universalizes: when the servant returns to reality, he knows that no creature has the power to create and originate; whoever attaches his heart to a created thing, or supposes in it a fragment of affirming or denying, resembles the worshippers of idols. Every dependence on other than God is a hidden idolatry.

mots.ar: يَا أَبَتِ لِمَ تَعْبُدُ مَا لَا يَسْمَعُ وَلَا يُبْصِرُ
mots.tr: yā abati lima taʿbudu mā lā yasmaʿu wa-lā yubṣiru
mots.racine: أ ب و / ع ب د / س م ع / ب ص ر
mots.sens: O father, why worship what hears not nor sees?

mots.racine: غ ن ي
mots.sens: and avails you nothing

versets.ar: يَا أَبَتِ إِنِّي قَدْ جَاءَنِي مِنَ الْعِلْمِ مَا لَمْ يَأْتِكَ فَاتَّبِعْنِي أَهْدِكَ صِرَاطًا سَوِيًّا
versets.tr: yā abati innī qad jāʾanī mina l-ʿilmi mā lam yaʾtika fa-ttabiʿnī ahdika ṣirāṭan sawiyyan
versets.trad: O my father! There has come to me of knowledge what has not come to you. So follow me: I will guide you to a level path.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he ordered him to follow because his side outweighed, the Truth being with him — though he was younger in age; and he showed that salvation lies in following the people of Truth, and ruin in innovation and straying in the misleadings of the paths. The son guides the father: not by insolence, but because true knowledge depends not on age but on gift. To follow the holder of the Truth, though younger, is the sure way.

mots.ar: قَدْ جَاءَنِي مِنَ الْعِلْمِ مَا لَمْ يَأْتِكَ
mots.tr: qad jāʾanī mina l-ʿilmi mā lam yaʾtika
mots.racine: ج ي أ / ع ل م
mots.sens: knowledge has come to me that came not to you

mots.racine: ت ب ع / ه د ي / ص ر ط
mots.sens: follow me, I will guide you to a level path

versets.ar: يَا أَبَتِ لَا تَعْبُدِ الشَّيْطَانَ إِنَّ الشَّيْطَانَ كَانَ لِلرَّحْمَٰنِ عَصِيًّا
versets.tr: yā abati lā taʿbudi sh-shayṭāna inna sh-shayṭāna kāna li-r-raḥmāni ʿaṣiyyan
versets.trad: O my father, do not worship Satan! Satan is indeed rebellious to the All-Merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he showed that the cause of the prohibition against worshipping Satan is his disobedience to the All-Merciful; it thus appeared that it in no way befits one to obey whoever disobeys God. And this maxim: the foundation of religion is to shun the masters of disobedience. To worship the idol is to obey the Satan who inspires it; and none deserves obedience who himself rebels against God.

mots.racine: ع ب د / ش ط ن
mots.sens: do not worship Satan

mots.racine: ر ح م / ع ص ي
mots.sens: he was rebellious to the All-Merciful

versets.ar: يَا أَبَتِ إِنِّي أَخَافُ أَن يَمَسَّكَ عَذَابٌ مِّنَ الرَّحْمَٰنِ فَتَكُونَ لِلشَّيْطَانِ وَلِيًّا
versets.tr: yā abati innī akhāfu an yamassaka ʿadhābun mina r-raḥmāni fa-takūna li-sh-shayṭāni waliyyan
versets.trad: O my father! I fear that a punishment from the All-Merciful will touch you, and that you will become an ally of Satan.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Intimate Friend (al-Khalīl, Abraham) neglected nothing of tenderness toward his father; yet neither the beauty of his exhortation availed nor the abundance of his counsel worked in him, for one whom the antecedents of the decree have set aside, later measures do not save. Abraham's fear is pure love: he dreads for his father the punishment of the All-Merciful. But grace is not forced, and the tenderest plea remains vain before a sealed heart.

mots.ar: أَخَافُ أَن يَمَسَّكَ عَذَابٌ مِّنَ الرَّحْمَٰنِ
mots.tr: akhāfu an yamassaka ʿadhābun mina r-raḥmāni
mots.racine: خ و ف / م س س / ع ذ ب
mots.sens: I fear a punishment from the All-Merciful will touch you

mots.racine: ش ط ن / و ل ي
mots.sens: and you become an ally of Satan

versets.ar: قَالَ أَرَاغِبٌ أَنتَ عَنْ آلِهَتِي يَا إِبْرَاهِيمُ لَئِن لَّمْ تَنتَهِ لَأَرْجُمَنَّكَ وَاهْجُرْنِي مَلِيًّا
versets.tr: qāla a-rāghibun anta ʿan ālihatī yā ibrāhīmu la-in lam tantahi la-arjumannaka wa-hjurnī maliyyan
versets.trad: He said: “Do you spurn my gods, O Abraham? If you do not cease, I shall surely stone you. Leave me for a long while.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī sums up the contrast: Abraham filled him with the promise of a fair outcome, and the father answered him with the threat of punishment: ‘if you do not cease, I shall stone you.’ To the gentleness of the son answers the violence of the father: to the offer of salvation, the threat of the stone. The contrast lights up the mystery of the foregoing verse — offered grace meets free refusal.

mots.racine: ر غ ب / أ ل ه
mots.sens: do you spurn my gods?
mots.def: rāghib ʿan: who turns away from.

mots.racine: ن ه ي / ر ج م
mots.sens: if you cease not, I shall stone you

versets.ar: قَالَ سَلَامٌ عَلَيْكَ سَأَسْتَغْفِرُ لَكَ رَبِّي إِنَّهُ كَانَ بِي حَفِيًّا
versets.tr: qāla salāmun ʿalayka sa-astaghfiru laka rabbī innahu kāna bī ḥafiyyan
versets.trad: Abraham said: “Peace be upon you! I shall ask forgiveness for you from my Lord. He is ever gracious to me.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Friend answered him according to the calm of clear-sight: ‘peace be upon you.’ He notes that this promise of intercession was before he despaired of his faith, while there yet remained in him a portion of hope on his behalf; but when he was certain that wretchedness was sealed upon him, he parted from him. The peace Abraham gives in return for the threat is the mark of the truthful: to insult he replies with greeting, and to rejection with prayer. He is gracious to me (ḥafiyy): his trust in God's gentleness grounds his own gentleness.

mots.racine: س ل م / غ ف ر
mots.sens: peace upon you, I shall seek forgiveness for you

mots.racine: ح ف و
mots.sens: He is gracious to me

versets.ar: وَأَعْتَزِلُكُمْ وَمَا تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ وَأَدْعُو رَبِّي عَسَىٰ أَلَّا أَكُونَ بِدُعَاءِ رَبِّي شَقِيًّا
versets.tr: wa-aʿtazilukum wa-mā tadʿūna min dūni llāhi wa-adʿū rabbī ʿasā allā akūna bi-duʿāʾi rabbī shaqiyyan
versets.trad: I withdraw from you and from what you call upon besides God. I call upon my Lord; perhaps I shall not be disappointed in my call to my Lord!”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī glosses briefly: ‘what you call upon’: what you worship; ‘I call upon my Lord’: I worship Him. Abraham carries out the separation (iʿtizāl): he withdraws from men and their idols to call upon none but the One. His “perhaps” is no doubt but humility: the ṣiddīq hopes without presuming, certain that the call to the true Lord never goes unanswered.

mots.ar: وَأَعْتَزِلُكُمْ وَمَا تَدْعُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ
mots.tr: wa-aʿtazilukum wa-mā tadʿūna min dūni llāhi
mots.racine: ع ز ل / د ع و
mots.sens: I withdraw from you and your idols
mots.def: iʿtazala: to withdraw; daʿā: to call upon, worship.

mots.ar: عَسَىٰ أَلَّا أَكُونَ بِدُعَاءِ رَبِّي شَقِيًّا
mots.tr: ʿasā allā akūna bi-duʿāʾi rabbī shaqiyyan
mots.racine: د ع و / ش ق و
mots.sens: perhaps I shall not be disappointed in calling Him

versets.ar: فَلَمَّا اعْتَزَلَهُمْ وَمَا يَعْبُدُونَ مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ وَهَبْنَا لَهُ إِسْحَاقَ وَيَعْقُوبَ وَكُلًّا جَعَلْنَا نَبِيًّا
versets.tr: fa-lammā ʿtazalahum wa-mā yaʿbudūna min dūni llāhi wahabnā lahu isḥāqa wa-yaʿqūba wa-kullan jaʿalnā nabiyyan
versets.trad: When he had withdrawn from them and from what they worshipped besides God, We granted him Isaac and Jacob, and each of them We made a prophet.
versets.commentaire: A pearl of Qushayrī: when he despaired of his root (his father), God consoled him with what He honoured him in his offspring: He made them grow in a fair growth, granted them prophethood, and the truthful remembrance that ceaselessly names them. He who leaves his kin for God is not impoverished but enriched: in place of the lost father, God gives a line of prophets. Renunciation for God never deprives: it exchanges the severed tie for a purer gift.

mots.racine: ع ز ل / ع ب د
mots.sens: when he withdrew from them and their idols

mots.racine: و ه ب
mots.sens: We granted him Isaac and Jacob

versets.ar: وَوَهَبْنَا لَهُم مِّن رَّحْمَتِنَا وَجَعَلْنَا لَهُمْ لِسَانَ صِدْقٍ عَلِيًّا
versets.tr: wa-wahabnā lahum min raḥmatinā wa-jaʿalnā lahum lisāna ṣidqin ʿaliyyan
versets.trad: And We granted them of Our mercy, and appointed for them a sublime tongue of truth.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī carries on from the foregoing gift: God granted them a mercy from Us and a high tongue of truth. He sees in it the blessed remembrance that perpetuates their name — perhaps, he suggests, the invocation of blessing upon Abraham and his house at the close of every prayer. The “sublime tongue of truth” (lisān ṣidq ʿaliyy) is the enduring praise God raises upon the lips of believers: the truthful one inherits the gift of being truthfully praised until the end of time.

mots.racine: و ه ب / ر ح م
mots.sens: We granted them of Our mercy

mots.racine: ج ع ل / ل س ن / ص د ق / ع ل و
mots.sens: We gave them a sublime tongue of truth
mots.def: lisān ṣidq: renown of truthfulness; ʿaliyy: high.

versets.ar: وَاذْكُرْ فِي الْكِتَابِ مُوسَىٰ إِنَّهُ كَانَ مُخْلَصًا وَكَانَ رَسُولًا نَّبِيًّا
versets.tr: wa-dhkur fī l-kitābi mūsā innahu kāna mukhlaṣan wa-kāna rasūlan nabiyyan
versets.trad: And mention in the Book Moses; he was a chosen one, and he was a messenger, a prophet.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī glosses “mukhlaṣ” (made pure, chosen): wholly for God, for none else in any way; the blame of no blamer seized him in God; no craving carried him toward the preference of an advantage; he closed his eyes, in God, to nothing. The chosen one (mukhlaṣ, in the passive) is not only he who purifies himself, but he whom God purifies: perfect sincerity is a gift before it is an effort.

mots.racine: خ ل ص
mots.sens: he was purified, chosen
mots.def: mukhlaṣ: made pure by God, chosen.

mots.racine: ر س ل / ن ب أ
mots.sens: he was a messenger, a prophet

versets.ar: وَنَادَيْنَاهُ مِن جَانِبِ الطُّورِ الْأَيْمَنِ وَقَرَّبْنَاهُ نَجِيًّا
versets.tr: wa-nādaynāhu min jānibi ṭ-ṭūri l-aymani wa-qarrabnāhu najiyyan
versets.trad: We called to him from the right side of the Mount, and drew him near in intimate converse.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: secret converse (najwā) has a merit over the call; He joined for Moses the two qualities: the call at its beginning, and hearing and converse at its end — the Real halted him, called him, and in both states took charge of him. He forestalls a misreading: ‘from the side of the Mount’ refers to Moses, who was at the side — not that the divine call came from a direction. Thus “We drew him near” is nearness of rank (qurb makāna), not of place. Moses' intimacy (najiyy) is the highest degree: to pass from the call to the confided whisper.

mots.racine: ن د و / ط و ر / ي م ن
mots.sens: We called him from the right side of the Mount

mots.racine: ق ر ب / ن ج و
mots.sens: We drew him near as a confidant
mots.def: najiyy: confidant, one who converses in secret.

versets.ar: وَوَهَبْنَا لَهُ مِن رَّحْمَتِنَا أَخَاهُ هَارُونَ نَبِيًّا
versets.tr: wa-wahabnā lahu min raḥmatinā akhāhu hārūna nabiyyan
versets.trad: And out of Our mercy We granted him his brother Aaron as a prophet.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, briefly: among the privileges proper to Moses, his brother Aaron was given to him as a prophet. Aaron's prophethood is presented as a mercy shown to Moses: God answered his plea for a support (“join him to my affair”). The gift of a prophet-brother shows that divine favour can extend through love, blessing not only the chosen one but those he loves.

mots.racine: و ه ب / ر ح م
mots.sens: out of Our mercy We granted him

mots.racine: أ خ و / ن ب أ
mots.sens: his brother Aaron as a prophet

versets.ar: وَاذْكُرْ فِي الْكِتَابِ إِسْمَاعِيلَ إِنَّهُ كَانَ صَادِقَ الْوَعْدِ وَكَانَ رَسُولًا نَّبِيًّا
versets.tr: wa-dhkur fī l-kitābi ismāʿīla innahu kāna ṣādiqa l-waʿdi wa-kāna rasūlan nabiyyan
versets.trad: And mention in the Book Ishmael; he was true to his promise, and he was a messenger, a prophet.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī ties Ishmael's faithfulness to the sacrifice: he was true to his promise, for he had pledged of himself patience under being slaughtered by his father, and he endured that until the ransom appeared. He was true because he kept the covenant. By this gloss, Qushayrī signals that in his view it is Ishmael — not Isaac — who is the child of the sacrifice. The “truthfulness of the promise” is the highest fidelity: to keep one's word even unto the offering of one's own life.

mots.racine: ص د ق / و ع د
mots.sens: he was true to his promise
mots.def: ṣādiq al-waʿd: truthful in his pledge.

mots.racine: ر س ل / ن ب أ
mots.sens: he was a messenger, a prophet

versets.ar: وَكَانَ يَأْمُرُ أَهْلَهُ بِالصَّلَاةِ وَالزَّكَاةِ وَكَانَ عِندَ رَبِّهِ مَرْضِيًّا
versets.tr: wa-kāna yaʾmuru ahlahu bi-ṣ-ṣalāti wa-z-zakāti wa-kāna ʿinda rabbihi marḍiyyan
versets.trad: He used to enjoin upon his people prayer and almsgiving, and he was pleasing to his Lord.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he enjoined upon his household prayer — by the command God gave him — and almsgiving; this includes what he commanded them of the worship of body and wealth, at all times and in every way. And he crowns it: ‘pleasing to his Lord’ (marḍiyy) was the noblest of his traits and the most sublime of his attributes. To command the good begins with one's own kin; and divine approval is the highest of stations.

mots.racine: أ م ر / أ ه ل / ص ل و / ز ك و
mots.sens: he enjoined on his people prayer and almsgiving

mots.racine: ر ض و
mots.sens: he was pleasing to his Lord

versets.ar: وَاذْكُرْ فِي الْكِتَابِ إِدْرِيسَ إِنَّهُ كَانَ صِدِّيقًا نَّبِيًّا
versets.tr: wa-dhkur fī l-kitābi idrīsa innahu kāna ṣiddīqan nabiyyan
versets.trad: And mention in the Book Idrīs (Enoch); he was a man of truth, a prophet,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī resumes the definition of the truthful one applied to Idrīs: the ṣiddīq is one whose sincerity abounds, without his authenticity being cut by any admixture; he stands by the Truth for the Truth, and there is in him no breath for other than God. The pure ṣiddīq has no ulterior aim: his whole being is strained toward the Real, with no portion reserved for the self.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر / ك ت ب
mots.sens: mention Idrīs in the Book

mots.racine: ص د ق / ن ب أ
mots.sens: he was truthful, a prophet

versets.ar: وَرَفَعْنَاهُ مَكَانًا عَلِيًّا
versets.tr: wa-rafaʿnāhu makānan ʿaliyyan
versets.trad: and We raised him to a sublime station.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, concise: ‘We raised him to a sublime place’: an immense degree in nurture, in which none equalled him. The raising (rafʿ) of Idrīs is a rank of nearness, the fruit of his truthfulness: whoever stands wholly for God, God lifts to a station none shares. The sublimity of the place answers the purity of the heart.

mots.racine: ر ف ع / ك و ن / ع ل و
mots.sens: We raised him to a sublime place
mots.def: rafaʿa: to raise; ʿaliyy: high, sublime.

versets.ar: أُولَٰئِكَ الَّذِينَ أَنْعَمَ اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِم مِّنَ النَّبِيِّينَ مِن ذُرِّيَّةِ آدَمَ وَمِمَّنْ حَمَلْنَا مَعَ نُوحٍ وَمِن ذُرِّيَّةِ إِبْرَاهِيمَ وَإِسْرَائِيلَ وَمِمَّنْ هَدَيْنَا وَاجْتَبَيْنَا إِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتُ الرَّحْمَٰنِ خَرُّوا سُجَّدًا وَبُكِيًّا
versets.tr: ulāʾika lladhīna anʿama llāhu ʿalayhim mina n-nabiyyīna min dhurriyyati ādama wa-mimman ḥamalnā maʿa nūḥin wa-min dhurriyyati ibrāhīma wa-isrāʾīla wa-mimman hadaynā wa-jtabaynā idhā tutlā ʿalayhim āyātu r-raḥmāni kharrū sujjadan wa-bukiyyan
versets.trad: These are they whom God has graced among the prophets, of the seed of Adam, and of those We carried with Noah, and of the seed of Abraham and Israel, and of those We guided and chose. When the signs of the All-Merciful were recited to them, they fell down prostrate, weeping.
versets.commentaire: The spiritual summit of the sūra. Qushayrī: He established them by the witnesses of union (jamʿ), and announced that His favour is hidden in His distinguishing them by their states and making them worthy of that to which He raised them; and that by His grace He chose and elected them. On their prostration in tears: among the privileges with which He graced them is the tenderness of their hearts: when the verses are recited to them, they prostrate; and the prostration of their bodies indicates the prostration of their secrets, by what He has realized for them of the witnesses of union. By the attribute of separation they fulfil the rights of servitude, and by the character of union they are realized in the realities of Lordship.

mots.racine: ن ع م
mots.sens: these whom God has graced

mots.ar: إِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتُ الرَّحْمَٰنِ خَرُّوا سُجَّدًا وَبُكِيًّا
mots.tr: idhā tutlā ʿalayhim āyātu r-raḥmāni kharrū sujjadan wa-bukiyyan
mots.racine: ت ل و / خ ر ر / س ج د / ب ك ي
mots.sens: at the All-Merciful's signs they fall prostrate, weeping
mots.def: kharra: to fall; sujjad: prostrate; bukiyy: weeping.

versets.ar: فَخَلَفَ مِن بَعْدِهِمْ خَلْفٌ أَضَاعُوا الصَّلَاةَ وَاتَّبَعُوا الشَّهَوَاتِ فَسَوْفَ يَلْقَوْنَ غَيًّا
versets.tr: fa-khalafa min baʿdihim khalfun aḍāʿū ṣ-ṣalāta wa-ttabaʿū sh-shahawāti fa-sawfa yalqawna ghayyan
versets.trad: But after them came successors who neglected the prayer and followed their desires; so they shall meet perdition,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: those who turned aside from their way, neglected the right of the Law, overstepped the duty of the command, deviated from the path of right guidance, failed the courtesies of the Law and threaded themselves into the train of desires — soon they will meet what they have merited, and be dealt with as they deserve. To the prostration of the elect is opposed the neglect (iḍāʿa) of prayer: to follow desires is to lose the station of service, and to arrive at nothing but perdition (ghayy).

mots.racine: ض ي ع / ص ل و / ت ب ع / ش ه و
mots.sens: they neglected prayer and followed desires
mots.def: aḍāʿa: to lose, neglect; shahawāt: desires.

mots.racine: ل ق ي / غ و ي
mots.sens: they will meet perdition

versets.ar: إِلَّا مَن تَابَ وَآمَنَ وَعَمِلَ صَالِحًا فَأُولَٰئِكَ يَدْخُلُونَ الْجَنَّةَ وَلَا يُظْلَمُونَ شَيْئًا
versets.tr: illā man tāba wa-āmana wa-ʿamila ṣāliḥan fa-ulāʾika yadkhulūna l-jannata wa-lā yuẓlamūna shayʾan
versets.trad: except those who repented, believed, and did righteous deeds: these shall enter the Garden and shall not be wronged in anything.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: these are they whom the pre-eternal mercy has overtaken; they will abide in everlasting delights. The Real fulfils for them His promises, brings them to their degrees, and realizes what He promised them. Repentance (tawba) reopens the door closed by neglect: whoever returns, believes, and acts well, the ancient grace claims him. None is wronged: justice gives the due, and mercy adds the surplus.

mots.ar: إِلَّا مَن تَابَ وَآمَنَ وَعَمِلَ صَالِحًا
mots.tr: illā man tāba wa-āmana wa-ʿamila ṣāliḥan
mots.racine: ت و ب / أ م ن / ع م ل / ص ل ح
mots.sens: except who repented, believed, did good
mots.def: tāba: to repent; ʿamila ṣāliḥan: to act well.

mots.racine: ظ ل م
mots.sens: they are not wronged in anything

versets.ar: جَنَّاتِ عَدْنٍ الَّتِي وَعَدَ الرَّحْمَٰنُ عِبَادَهُ بِالْغَيْبِ إِنَّهُ كَانَ وَعْدُهُ مَأْتِيًّا
versets.tr: jannāti ʿadnin allatī waʿada r-raḥmānu ʿibādahu bi-l-ghaybi innahu kāna waʿduhu maʾtiyyan
versets.trad: Gardens of Eden, which the All-Merciful has promised His servants in the Unseen. His promise is ever fulfilled.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī plays on the reciprocity of “maʾtiyy”: ‘His promise has come to pass’ — for that toward which you go has come to you, and that which comes to you, it is you who go to it. The promise made “in the Unseen” (bi-l-ghayb) — invisible, on faith alone — is infallibly kept: what the All-Merciful has promised in the unseen unfailingly comes about. Faith in the absent is honoured by the coming of the promised.

mots.ar: جَنَّاتِ عَدْنٍ الَّتِي وَعَدَ الرَّحْمَٰنُ عِبَادَهُ بِالْغَيْبِ
mots.tr: jannāti ʿadnin allatī waʿada r-raḥmānu ʿibādahu bi-l-ghaybi
mots.racine: ج ن ن / ع د ن / و ع د / غ ي ب
mots.sens: Gardens of Eden promised in the Unseen
mots.def: ʿadn: permanent abode; ghayb: the unseen.

mots.racine: و ع د / أ ت ي
mots.sens: His promise unfailingly comes to pass
mots.def: maʾtiyy: that which comes, is attained.

versets.ar: لَّا يَسْمَعُونَ فِيهَا لَغْوًا إِلَّا سَلَامًا وَلَهُمْ رِزْقُهُمْ فِيهَا بُكْرَةً وَعَشِيًّا
versets.tr: lā yasmaʿūna fīhā laghwan illā salāman wa-lahum rizquhum fīhā bukratan wa-ʿashiyyan
versets.trad: They hear no idle talk there, only “Peace”; and they have their provision there morning and evening.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī spiritualizes the hearing of the elect: their ears are preserved from hearing strangers; they hear only from God and by God; and if not that, they hear only God. On the provision “morning and evening,” he recalls the humble earthly state of the saints, often deprived of one of the two meals, then distinguishes the provisions of the Garden: for the bodies a provision of food and drink, for the spirits a provision of hearing and contemplation — to each, by his merit, a known portion.

mots.ar: لَّا يَسْمَعُونَ فِيهَا لَغْوًا إِلَّا سَلَامًا
mots.tr: lā yasmaʿūna fīhā laghwan illā salāman
mots.racine: س م ع / ل غ و / س ل م
mots.sens: they hear no idle talk, only ‘Peace’

mots.racine: ر ز ق / ب ك ر / ع ش و
mots.sens: their provision morning and evening

versets.ar: تِلْكَ الْجَنَّةُ الَّتِي نُورِثُ مِنْ عِبَادِنَا مَن كَانَ تَقِيًّا
versets.tr: tilka l-jannatu llatī nūrithu min ʿibādinā man kāna taqiyyan
versets.trad: Such is the Garden which We give as inheritance to those of Our servants who were God-fearing.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī distinguishes Garden and mercy: the Garden is prepared for the pious of this community; mercy is reserved for the sinners among the Muslims. The Garden is a grace of God; mercy is an attribute of God. Then he grades piety (taqwā): one people fears faults and transgressions, another fears desires, another fears inadvertences, another still fears contemplating any other than Him. The highest taqwā is to see none but God.

mots.racine: ج ن ن / و ر ث
mots.sens: such is the Garden We give in inheritance

mots.racine: ع ب د / و ق ي
mots.sens: those of Our servants who were pious

versets.ar: وَمَا نَتَنَزَّلُ إِلَّا بِأَمْرِ رَبِّكَ لَهُ مَا بَيْنَ أَيْدِينَا وَمَا خَلْفَنَا وَمَا بَيْنَ ذَٰلِكَ وَمَا كَانَ رَبُّكَ نَسِيًّا
versets.tr: wa-mā natanazzalu illā bi-amri rabbika lahu mā bayna aydīnā wa-mā khalfanā wa-mā bayna dhālika wa-mā kāna rabbuka nasiyyan
versets.trad: [The angels say:] “We descend only by the command of your Lord. To Him belongs what is before us and what is behind us and what is between. Your Lord is never forgetful!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the angels descend always by the permission of the Real: some to succour the wronged, some to aid the afflicted, some to destroy the deniers, some to support the believers, others for countless affairs of all mankind. And he concludes: God leaves neither a denier nor a worshipper without preservation and bounty, or respite and chastisement. “Your Lord is not forgetful”: none escapes His vigilance, neither the elect nor the rebel.

mots.racine: ن ز ل / أ م ر
mots.sens: we descend only by your Lord's command

mots.racine: ن س ي
mots.sens: your Lord is never forgetful

versets.ar: رَّبُّ السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَمَا بَيْنَهُمَا فَاعْبُدْهُ وَاصْطَبِرْ لِعِبَادَتِهِ هَلْ تَعْلَمُ لَهُ سَمِيًّا
versets.tr: rabbu s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi wa-mā baynahumā fa-ʿbudhu wa-ṣṭabir li-ʿibādatihi hal taʿlamu lahu samiyyan
versets.trad: Lord of the heavens and the earth and all between them: so worship Him, and persevere in His worship. Do you know any namesake for Him?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī makes of “worship Him” a call to surrender: stand where He has commanded you, leave what befalls you, abandon your opinion and your planning. And “persevere in His worship”: iṣṭibār is the utmost of patience. On “do you know for Him a namesake?”: do you know a single one named ‘Allāh’ besides Allāh? Whence a peer, He being unique by eternity? Likeness requires equality between likes, and He has no like, neither existent nor even imagined.

mots.racine: ع ب د / ص ب ر
mots.sens: worship Him and persevere in His worship

mots.racine: س م و
mots.sens: do you know for Him a namesake?

versets.ar: وَيَقُولُ الْإِنسَانُ أَإِذَا مَا مِتُّ لَسَوْفَ أُخْرَجُ حَيًّا
versets.tr: wa-yaqūlu l-insānu a-idhā mā mittu la-sawfa ukhraju ḥayyan
versets.trad: Man says: “When I am dead, shall I be brought forth alive?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they denied to the utmost the account of resurrection; God set up the proof against them by the first creation. Man's doubt about his own resurrection is refuted by his very origin: He who drew him from nothing can bring him back to life. The argument is the hallmark of sūra Qāf, here resumed: the second creation is but a repeat of the first.

mots.racine: ق و ل / أ ن س
mots.sens: man says

mots.ar: أَإِذَا مَا مِتُّ لَسَوْفَ أُخْرَجُ حَيًّا
mots.tr: a-idhā mā mittu la-sawfa ukhraju ḥayyan
mots.racine: م و ت / خ ر ج / ح ي ي
mots.sens: once dead, shall I be brought forth alive?
mots.def: ukhraja: to be brought out; ḥayy: alive.

versets.ar: أَوَلَا يَذْكُرُ الْإِنسَانُ أَنَّا خَلَقْنَاهُ مِن قَبْلُ وَلَمْ يَكُ شَيْئًا
versets.tr: a-wa-lā yadhkuru l-insānu annā khalaqnāhu min qablu wa-lam yaku shayʾan
versets.trad: Does man not remember that We created him before, when he was nothing?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He who was able to create the creatures at the beginning — when they were weak drops, and before that in the loins of fathers and the wombs of mothers — fashioned and formed them as He willed, and at the moment He willed brought them forth from their mothers' wombs. On “he was nothing”: a proof, for the people of insight, that pure non-being is not a thing in its state of non-existence. To remember one's origin of nothingness ruins all pretension to deny resurrection.

mots.racine: ذ ك ر
mots.sens: does man not remember?

mots.ar: أَنَّا خَلَقْنَاهُ مِن قَبْلُ وَلَمْ يَكُ شَيْئًا
mots.tr: annā khalaqnāhu min qablu wa-lam yaku shayʾan
mots.racine: خ ل ق / ش ي أ
mots.sens: that We created him when he was nothing

versets.ar: فَوَرَبِّكَ لَنَحْشُرَنَّهُمْ وَالشَّيَاطِينَ ثُمَّ لَنُحْضِرَنَّهُمْ حَوْلَ جَهَنَّمَ جِثِيًّا
versets.tr: fa-wa-rabbika la-naḥshurannahum wa-sh-shayāṭīna thumma la-nuḥḍirannahum ḥawla jahannama jithiyyan
versets.trad: By your Lord! We shall surely gather them and the devils, then bring them around Hell on their knees.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: We shall gather them all, and they will assemble on the plain; then their destinies will diverge: one people to the Fire, to degrees lower one than another — the name of Hell gathers their places; one people to the Garden, in degrees higher one than another — the name of Garden embraces their dwellings. And this remark: the gap between the degrees of the Garden is greater than the gap between the inhabitants of the two abodes. Kneeling (jithiyy) around the fire: the posture of the terror of the reckoning.

mots.racine: ر ب ب / ح ش ر / ش ط ن
mots.sens: by your Lord, We shall gather them with the devils

mots.racine: ح ض ر / ج ه ن م / ج ث و
mots.sens: then bring them kneeling around Hell

versets.ar: ثُمَّ لَنَنزِعَنَّ مِن كُلِّ شِيعَةٍ أَيُّهُمْ أَشَدُّ عَلَى الرَّحْمَٰنِ عِتِيًّا
versets.tr: thumma la-nanziʿanna min kulli shīʿatin ayyuhum ashaddu ʿalā r-raḥmāni ʿitiyyan
versets.trad: Then We shall pluck out from every faction those most insolent toward the All-Merciful,
themes: the singling of the most rebellious
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: he who outstripped the others in error and in leading astray will see doubled upon him, tomorrow, the punishment and the chains. From each faction the most insolent is dragged forth: requital follows the intensity of revolt. The stronger the rebellion (ʿutuww) against the All-Merciful, the more remote from God the place in the Fire, and the more crushing.

mots.racine: ن ز ع / ش ي ع
mots.sens: We shall pluck out from every faction
mots.def: nazaʿa: to pluck out; shīʿa: faction.

mots.racine: ش د د / ع ت و
mots.sens: the most insolent toward the All-Merciful

versets.ar: ثُمَّ لَنَحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِالَّذِينَ هُمْ أَوْلَىٰ بِهَا صِلِيًّا
versets.tr: thumma la-naḥnu aʿlamu bi-lladhīna hum awlā bihā ṣiliyyan
versets.trad: then We know best those most deserving to burn in it.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: into each degree of Hell descends the one who deserves it; he whose insolence today was the most excessive will be in the Fire the most remote from God, and the most harshly punished and abased. The divine knowledge assigns to each his rank in the punishment according to the truth of his state: no injustice, but the just measure of what each has merited of burning (ṣiliyy).

mots.racine: ع ل م
mots.sens: We know best those who

mots.racine: و ل ي / ص ل ي
mots.sens: are most deserving to burn in it

versets.ar: وَإِن مِّنكُمْ إِلَّا وَارِدُهَا كَانَ عَلَىٰ رَبِّكَ حَتْمًا مَّقْضِيًّا
versets.tr: wa-in minkum illā wāriduhā kāna ʿalā rabbika ḥatman maqḍiyyan
versets.trad: There is none of you but shall come to it: that is a decree fixed by your Lord.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: each one comes to the Fire, but none suffers from it harm or detention save in the measure of his faults and lapses; the most sunk in fault burns there the most. He reports this saying: ‘the Fire, at the passing of some, freezes as milk freezes; they enter it without feeling it, and having crossed it say: were we not promised Hell on the way? They are told: you have crossed it without being aware!’ The passing that terrifies becomes, for the believer, coolness and peace, like Abraham's fire.

mots.racine: و ر د
mots.sens: none of you but comes to it
mots.def: wārid: one who comes to, arrives at.

mots.racine: ح ت م / ق ض ي
mots.sens: a sentence decreed by your Lord

versets.ar: ثُمَّ نُنَجِّي الَّذِينَ اتَّقَوا وَّنَذَرُ الظَّالِمِينَ فِيهَا جِثِيًّا
versets.tr: thumma nunajjī lladhīna ttaqaw wa-nadharu ẓ-ẓālimīna fīhā jithiyyan
versets.trad: Then We shall save those who feared God, and leave the wrongdoers there on their knees.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He saves whoever was a believer, some before others; but no believer remains without being saved. The deniers are left, marked by the despair of not coming out; then the trial deepens upon them, the gates of Hell close, and hope and expectation are cut off in them. And: people are saved only in the measure of their piety: the increase of taqwā hastens their salvation — one outstrips, one follows, one lingers, one burns. Salvation is graded as fear is graded.

mots.racine: ن ج و / و ق ي
mots.sens: then We save those who feared God
mots.def: najjā: to save; ittaqā: to be pious.

mots.racine: و ذ ر / ظ ل م / ج ث و
mots.sens: We leave the wrongdoers there on their knees

versets.ar: وَإِذَا تُتْلَىٰ عَلَيْهِمْ آيَاتُنَا بَيِّنَاتٍ قَالَ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا لِلَّذِينَ آمَنُوا أَيُّ الْفَرِيقَيْنِ خَيْرٌ مَّقَامًا وَأَحْسَنُ نَدِيًّا
versets.tr: wa-idhā tutlā ʿalayhim āyātunā bayyinātin qāla lladhīna kafarū lilladhīna āmanū ayyu l-farīqayni khayrun maqāman wa-aḥsanu nadiyyan
versets.trad: When Our clear signs are recited to them, those who disbelieve say to those who believe: “Which of the two parties has the better standing and the finer company?”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when the verses of the Qurʾān are recited to them, they meet them with rejection, denial, insolence and deviation; they claim to be upon the truth, relying in this on nothing but conjecture and opinion. Measuring worth by the standard of worldly comfort and rank, the deniers think themselves superior: their “better standing” is illusion, for outward splendour says nothing of inner truth.

mots.racine: ت ل و / أ ي ي / ب ي ن
mots.sens: when Our clear signs are recited to them

mots.ar: أَيُّ الْفَرِيقَيْنِ خَيْرٌ مَّقَامًا وَأَحْسَنُ نَدِيًّا
mots.tr: ayyu l-farīqayni khayrun maqāman wa-aḥsanu nadiyyan
mots.racine: ف ر ق / ق و م / ن د و
mots.sens: which party has better standing and assembly?

versets.ar: وَكَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا قَبْلَهُم مِّن قَرْنٍ هُمْ أَحْسَنُ أَثَاثًا وَرِئْيًا
versets.tr: wa-kam ahlaknā qablahum min qarnin hum aḥsanu athāthan wa-riʾyan
versets.trad: How many a generation We destroyed before them, who surpassed them in goods and in splendour!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: these thread themselves into the train of their predecessors; as they followed their path in doubt, they will meet what they merit for the foulness of their works. The past generations surpassed the present in furnishings (athāth) and fair appearance (riʾy), and yet were destroyed: material prosperity is no pledge of salvation, and the splendour of goods did not save the perished peoples.

mots.racine: ه ل ك / ق ر ن
mots.sens: how many generations destroyed before them

mots.racine: أ ث ث / ر أ ي
mots.sens: better in goods and in appearance

versets.ar: قُلْ مَن كَانَ فِي الضَّلَالَةِ فَلْيَمْدُدْ لَهُ الرَّحْمَٰنُ مَدًّا حَتَّىٰ إِذَا رَأَوْا مَا يُوعَدُونَ إِمَّا الْعَذَابَ وَإِمَّا السَّاعَةَ فَسَيَعْلَمُونَ مَنْ هُوَ شَرٌّ مَّكَانًا وَأَضْعَفُ جُندًا
versets.tr: qul man kāna fī ḍ-ḍalālati fa-l-yamdud lahu r-raḥmānu maddan ḥattā idhā raʾaw mā yūʿadūna immā l-ʿadhāba wa-immā s-sāʿata fa-sa-yaʿlamūna man huwa sharrun makānan wa-aḍʿafu jundan
versets.trad: Say: “Let the All-Merciful prolong the life of whoever is in error, until, when they see what they were promised — either the punishment or the Hour — they shall know who is worst in station and weakest in host.”
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: God grants respite to the deniers that they may lean on the vanities of their conjectures and be deluded by the quiet of their state; they forget Him in the heedlessness of the reprieve and the delusion of well-being, then the decree envelops them with what ruins their reckoning. The respite (madd) is no favour but a trial: the comfort that endures lulls the heart until the hour when the truth of the punishment is uncovered.

mots.racine: م د د / ر ح م
mots.sens: let the All-Merciful grant him long respite
mots.def: madda: to prolong, grant a reprieve.

mots.ar: مَنْ هُوَ شَرٌّ مَّكَانًا وَأَضْعَفُ جُندًا
mots.tr: man huwa sharrun makānan wa-aḍʿafu jundan
mots.racine: ش ر ر / ض ع ف / ج ن د
mots.sens: who is worse in place and weaker in host

versets.ar: وَيَزِيدُ اللَّهُ الَّذِينَ اهْتَدَوْا هُدًى وَالْبَاقِيَاتُ الصَّالِحَاتُ خَيْرٌ عِندَ رَبِّكَ ثَوَابًا وَخَيْرٌ مَّرَدًّا
versets.tr: wa-yazīdu llāhu lladhīna htadaw hudan wa-l-bāqiyātu ṣ-ṣāliḥātu khayrun ʿinda rabbika thawāban wa-khayrun maraddan
versets.trad: God increases in guidance those who are guided; and the enduring righteous deeds are better with your Lord in reward and better in return.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He enriches them with the light of the full moon so that they no longer need the gleam of the star, then with the breaking of dawn before the rising of the sun; and when the day of gnosis blossoms, there is no darkness and no suspicion. On “the enduring deeds”: the attestation of Lordship is better than any other work in which the sincerity of intention is absent. The increase of guidance carries one from sure knowledge to sure vision, then to the truth of certainty.

mots.racine: ز ي د / ه د ي
mots.sens: God increases the guided in guidance
mots.def: zāda: to increase; ihtadā: to be guided.

mots.racine: ب ق ي / ص ل ح
mots.sens: the enduring good deeds are better

versets.ar: أَفَرَأَيْتَ الَّذِي كَفَرَ بِآيَاتِنَا وَقَالَ لَأُوتَيَنَّ مَالًا وَوَلَدًا
versets.tr: a-fa-raʾayta lladhī kafara bi-āyātinā wa-qāla la-ūtayanna mālan wa-waladan
versets.trad: Have you seen the one who disbelieved in Our signs and said: “I shall surely be given wealth and children”?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: He recounts the story of that denier who swore — without proof — that he would be given wealth and children, and who supposed his oath would be verified. The presumption of the disbeliever is to measure divine favour by his own ease, and to take for granted what depends only on the decree. His oath has no ground but pride: he disposes of the future as though he were its master.

mots.racine: ر أ ي / ك ف ر / أ ي ي
mots.sens: have you seen him who denied Our signs?

mots.racine: أ ت ي / م و ل / و ل د
mots.sens: and said: I shall be given wealth and children
mots.def: ūtiya: to be given; māl: wealth.

versets.ar: أَطَّلَعَ الْغَيْبَ أَمِ اتَّخَذَ عِندَ الرَّحْمَٰنِ عَهْدًا
versets.tr: aṭṭalaʿa l-ghayba ami ttakhadha ʿinda r-raḥmāni ʿahdan
versets.trad: Has he gained access to the Unseen, or taken a covenant with the All-Merciful?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from this denunciation a gleam of hope for the believer: the implication of the words is that if the believer thinks well of God, or hopes from Him many things, God realizes them for him and verifies his expectation — for he is in covenant with God, and God does not break His covenant. Where the presumptuous one is belied, the trusting believer is answered: the difference lies not in the boldness of hope, but in the covenant (ʿahd) that grounds it.

mots.racine: ط ل ع / غ ي ب
mots.sens: has he accessed the Unseen?
mots.def: iṭṭalaʿa: to gain access to; ghayb: the hidden.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / ع ه د
mots.sens: or has he a covenant with the All-Merciful?

versets.ar: كَلَّا سَنَكْتُبُ مَا يَقُولُ وَنَمُدُّ لَهُ مِنَ الْعَذَابِ مَدًّا
versets.tr: kallā sa-naktubu mā yaqūlu wa-namuddu lahu mina l-ʿadhābi maddan
versets.trad: No! We shall record what he says, and prolong for him the punishment at length.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: no, the matter is not as he says, and their words have no truth; rather We shall prolong for them the punishment at length. To the deceiving respite of this world answers the prolonging of punishment in the next: what he held to be a guaranteed favour becomes a register against him. Presumption turns into extended pain.

mots.racine: ك ت ب / ق و ل
mots.sens: no! We shall record what he says
mots.def: kallā: by no means; kataba: to record.

mots.racine: م د د / ع ذ ب
mots.sens: We shall prolong for him the punishment

versets.ar: وَنَرِثُهُ مَا يَقُولُ وَيَأْتِينَا فَرْدًا
versets.tr: wa-narithuhu mā yaqūlu wa-yaʾtīnā fardan
