versets.trad: We shall inherit from him what he claims, and he will come to Us alone.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: We shall not let him enjoy his children, his retinue, his servants and his people; he will return to Us, alone, severed from them. All in which he gloried — wealth and children — God inherits, and the man appears stripped. The solitude (fard) of the reckoning is the final rebuttal of one who thought himself provided for: no retinue accompanies to the rendezvous.

mots.racine: و ر ث / ق و ل
mots.sens: We inherit what he claims to own

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ف ر د
mots.sens: he comes to Us alone

versets.ar: وَاتَّخَذُوا مِن دُونِ اللَّهِ آلِهَةً لِّيَكُونُوا لَهُمْ عِزًّا
versets.tr: wa-ttakhadhū min dūni llāhi ālihatan li-yakūnū lahum ʿizzan
versets.trad: They have taken gods besides God, that they might be a might for them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: they judged, by their corrupt conjecture, that their idols would protect them, and that what they worshipped besides God would earn them, with God, an intercession — a chimera! And this point: they sought glory (ʿizz) in the places of abasement; they failed in their quest and were driven from their aim. To seek strength other than in the One is to lose it: the idol is but impotence in disguise.

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / أ ل ه
mots.sens: they took gods besides God

mots.racine: ع ز ز
mots.sens: that they be for them a might

versets.ar: كَلَّا سَيَكْفُرُونَ بِعِبَادَتِهِمْ وَيَكُونُونَ عَلَيْهِمْ ضِدًّا
versets.tr: kallā sa-yakfurūna bi-ʿibādatihim wa-yakūnūna ʿalayhim ḍiddan
versets.trad: No! They will deny their worship and will become adversaries against them.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: when they are gathered and their idols gathered, the idols will disown them; and the profit they hoped from them will turn into harm against them. What is worshipped besides God turns upon its worshipper on the Day of reckoning: the idol, far from defending, accuses. The might sought becomes adversity (ḍidd) — the object of the cult disowns the cult once rendered to it.

mots.racine: ك ف ر / ع ب د
mots.sens: no! they will deny their worship

mots.racine: ض د د
mots.sens: and be against them adversaries

versets.ar: أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّا أَرْسَلْنَا الشَّيَاطِينَ عَلَى الْكَافِرِينَ تَؤُزُّهُمْ أَزًّا
versets.tr: a-lam tara annā arsalnā sh-shayāṭīna ʿalā l-kāfirīna taʾuzzuhum azzan
versets.trad: Do you not see that We send the devils against the disbelievers, inciting them relentlessly?
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī draws from this verse a criterion of spiritual discernment: ‘inciting them’: troubling them; for the suggestion of the devil (khāṭir) is accompanied by trouble and anguish, while the suggestion of the Real is accompanied by repose and quiet — this is one of the signs that distinguish them. An agitated thought: mark of the devil. A calmed thought: mark of the Real. Incitement to evil is known by the unease it sows.

mots.racine: ر س ل / ش ط ن / ك ف ر
mots.sens: We sent the devils against the disbelievers

mots.racine: أ ز ز
mots.sens: inciting them relentlessly

versets.ar: فَلَا تَعْجَلْ عَلَيْهِمْ إِنَّمَا نَعُدُّ لَهُمْ عَدًّا
versets.tr: fa-lā taʿjal ʿalayhim innamā naʿuddu lahum ʿaddan
versets.trad: So do not be hasty against them; We are only counting out for them a count.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the breaths are counted in the decree; whoever has not exhausted his measure, his measure does not end. When the term comes, no stratagem avails thereafter; and before its term, no accident increases it or diminishes it. Not to hasten against the deniers is to entrust oneself to the divine count: God counts their breaths one by one, and every life ends at the appointed instant, neither before nor after.

mots.racine: ع ج ل
mots.sens: do not be hasty against them

mots.racine: ع د د
mots.sens: We count out for them a count

versets.ar: يَوْمَ نَحْشُرُ الْمُتَّقِينَ إِلَى الرَّحْمَٰنِ وَفْدًا
versets.tr: yawma naḥshuru l-muttaqīna ilā r-raḥmāni wafdan
versets.trad: The Day We gather the godfearing to the All-Merciful as an honoured delegation,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī, magnificent, declines the mounts of the pious: it is said: as riders upon the noble mounts of their acts of obedience; and they differ — one mounted on the forefront of his obediences, one on the mounts of his aspirations, one on the noble beasts of his lights; and another carried, whom the Real carries in his afterlife as He carries him today in his life. And the one carried by the Real is not like the one carried by creatures! The pious come as a delegation of honour (wafd), mounted on their own virtues.

mots.racine: ح ش ر / و ق ي
mots.sens: the Day We gather the godfearing

mots.racine: ر ح م / و ف د
mots.sens: to the All-Merciful as a delegation

versets.ar: وَنَسُوقُ الْمُجْرِمِينَ إِلَىٰ جَهَنَّمَ وِرْدًا
versets.tr: wa-nasūqu l-mujrimīna ilā jahannama wirdan
versets.trad: and drive the guilty to Hell like a thirsty herd,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī contrasts the two convoys: those are led with the attribute of glory, these with the character of abasement; the word ‘drive’ unites them, but parts them in meaning — what a distance between the two! The pious ride as a delegation, the guilty are driven like thirsty beasts (wird) to Hell. The same verb covers two opposite destinies: honour on the one side, debasement on the other.

mots.racine: س و ق / ج ر م
mots.sens: We drive the guilty

mots.racine: ج ه ن م / و ر د
mots.sens: to Hell as to a watering-place
mots.def: wird: a thirsty herd led to water.

versets.ar: لَّا يَمْلِكُونَ الشَّفَاعَةَ إِلَّا مَنِ اتَّخَذَ عِندَ الرَّحْمَٰنِ عَهْدًا
versets.tr: lā yamlikūna sh-shafāʿata illā mani ttakhadha ʿinda r-raḥmāni ʿahdan
versets.trad: They will have no power of intercession, save one who has taken a covenant with the All-Merciful.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: that covenant is what guarded them in their world: the pledge taken from them on the Day of the Covenant (mīthāq), to stand by the testimony of the oneness of their Master. Intercession belongs only to one who holds a covenant (ʿahd) with the All-Merciful — that primordial covenant of oneness, sealed before creation, and kept throughout life by the attestation of tawḥīd.

mots.racine: م ل ك / ش ف ع
mots.sens: they have no power of intercession

mots.ar: إِلَّا مَنِ اتَّخَذَ عِندَ الرَّحْمَٰنِ عَهْدًا
mots.tr: illā mani ttakhadha ʿinda r-raḥmāni ʿahdan
mots.racine: أ خ ذ / ع ه د
mots.sens: save who has a covenant with the All-Merciful

versets.ar: وَقَالُوا اتَّخَذَ الرَّحْمَٰنُ وَلَدًا
versets.tr: wa-qālū ttakhadha r-raḥmānu waladan
versets.trad: They say: “The All-Merciful has taken a son!”
themes: the blasphemy of the attributed son
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī is indignant: what an enormity in their saying! what audacity in the foulness of their state! To attribute a son to the All-Merciful is the height of falsehood, broached here as the summit of the sūra's error. The very Name of Raḥmān, infinite gentleness, makes the insult more glaring: one ascribes to the Source of mercy a lack that would call for procreation.

mots.racine: ق و ل / أ خ ذ / و ل د
mots.sens: they said: the All-Merciful has taken a son
mots.def: ittakhadha waladan: to take a child.

versets.ar: لَّقَدْ جِئْتُمْ شَيْئًا إِدًّا
versets.tr: laqad jiʾtum shayʾan iddan
versets.trad: You have indeed put forth a monstrous thing,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī continues his cry of horror before the blasphemy: the thing uttered is “idd” — monstrous, enormous. He stresses that this word defiles only those who speak it: the absolute purity of the Ṣamadiyya is too holy for any evil to return to it, whether from one who affirms His oneness or from one who darkens Him by his heresy; it is only their faces that are made ugly by what they plunged into in their words.

mots.racine: ج ي أ / أ د د
mots.sens: you have put forth a monstrous thing

versets.ar: تَكَادُ السَّمَاوَاتُ يَتَفَطَّرْنَ مِنْهُ وَتَنشَقُّ الْأَرْضُ وَتَخِرُّ الْجِبَالُ هَدًّا
versets.tr: takādu s-samāwātu yatafaṭṭarna minhu wa-tanshaqqu l-arḍu wa-takhirru l-jibālu haddan
versets.trad: at which the heavens are nearly rent, the earth split open, and the mountains collapse in ruin,
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the Ṣamadiyya (the divine absoluteness) remains holy, untouched by any return of glory or defilement; this blasphemy makes ugly only those who commit it, and returns only upon the speaker, in this world or the next. The horror of the saying is such that the whole creation nearly breaks apart: heavens rent, earth cracked, mountains fallen. The universe shudders at the insult to transcendence, but the Absolute remains unscathed.

mots.racine: ك و د / س م و / ف ط ر
mots.sens: the heavens nearly rend at it

mots.racine: خ ر ر / ج ب ل / ه د د
mots.sens: the mountains collapse in ruin

versets.ar: أَن دَعَوْا لِلرَّحْمَٰنِ وَلَدًا
versets.tr: an daʿaw li-r-raḥmāni waladan
versets.trad: that they ascribed a son to the All-Merciful,
versets.commentaire: This verse gives the cause of the cosmic shaking: to have claimed (daʿaw) a son for the All-Merciful. Qushayrī, in his grouped commentary, recalls that the insult does not touch the Absolute but falls back upon the offender: transcendence remains pure, and it is the blasphemer's face that blackens with his own error. The Name of Raḥmān, invoked here again, brings out the absurdity: Mercy has need of no heir.

mots.racine: د ع و / ر ح م / و ل د
mots.sens: that they ascribed a son to the All-Merciful

versets.ar: وَمَا يَنبَغِي لِلرَّحْمَٰنِ أَن يَتَّخِذَ وَلَدًا
versets.tr: wa-mā yanbaghī li-r-raḥmāni an yattakhidha waladan
versets.trad: when it does not befit the All-Merciful to take a son!
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: how a son, when He is the One? how begetting, when He has no congener, neither by necessity nor by possibility? Begetting supposes a like, a parity; but God has no peer, neither real nor conceivable. It does not “befit” (mā yanbaghī) the All-Merciful to have a son, not from any lack of power, but because it contradicts His very oneness.

mots.racine: ب غ ي / ر ح م
mots.sens: it does not befit the All-Merciful

mots.racine: أ خ ذ / و ل د
mots.sens: to take a son
mots.def: ittakhadha waladan: to take a child.

versets.ar: إِن كُلُّ مَن فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ إِلَّا آتِي الرَّحْمَٰنِ عَبْدًا
versets.tr: in kullu man fī s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi illā ātī r-raḥmāni ʿabdan
versets.trad: There is none in the heavens and the earth but comes to the All-Merciful as a servant.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī includes this verse in his refutation: all who people the heavens and the earth come to the All-Merciful as a servant (ʿabd). None, be he prophet or angel, escapes the condition of servitude: sonship would suppose a parity with God, whereas every creature, without exception, is before Him but a slave. Universal servitude abolishes all pretension to a divine offspring.

mots.ar: إِن كُلُّ مَن فِي السَّمَاوَاتِ وَالْأَرْضِ
mots.tr: in kullu man fī s-samāwāti wa-l-arḍi
mots.racine: س م و / أ ر ض
mots.sens: all who are in the heavens and earth

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ع ب د
mots.sens: but come to the All-Merciful as servants
mots.def: ātī: one who comes; ʿabd: servant.

versets.ar: لَّقَدْ أَحْصَاهُمْ وَعَدَّهُمْ عَدًّا
versets.tr: laqad aḥṣāhum wa-ʿaddahum ʿaddan
versets.trad: He has indeed numbered them and counted them precisely.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: ‘He has numbered them’: no known thing escapes His knowledge, and nothing of which one may say it comes to be — even a thing merely imagined — detaches from His power. The divine numbering (iḥṣāʾ) embraces every creature: God knows and counts each one individually. This exhaustive knowledge is itself an argument against sonship: He who numbers all has need of nothing, and none is akin to Him.

mots.racine: ح ص ي
mots.sens: He has numbered them

mots.racine: ع د د
mots.sens: and counted them a count

versets.ar: وَكُلُّهُمْ آتِيهِ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ فَرْدًا
versets.tr: wa-kulluhum ātīhi yawma l-qiyāmati fardan
versets.trad: Each of them will come to Him on the Day of Resurrection, alone.
themes: the solitude of the Last Day
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: ‘each comes to Him, alone, on the Day of resurrection’: no servant accompanies him, no retinue joins him; each is occupied only with himself, severed from all other. The solitude (fard) of the Last Day answers the individual numbering: counted one by one, each appears one by one. No cortège, no ally — only the deed precedes, and only the covenant of oneness accompanies.

mots.racine: أ ت ي / ق و م
mots.sens: each comes to Him on the Day of Resurrection

mots.racine: ف ر د
mots.sens: alone

versets.ar: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحَاتِ سَيَجْعَلُ لَهُمُ الرَّحْمَٰنُ وُدًّا
versets.tr: inna lladhīna āmanū wa-ʿamilū ṣ-ṣāliḥāti sa-yajʿalu lahumu r-raḥmānu wuddan
versets.trad: Those who believe and do righteous deeds — the All-Merciful will appoint for them love.
versets.commentaire: Spiritual seal of the sūra. Qushayrī: He sets in their hearts a love for God, the fruit of their pure works, and he cites the holy ḥadīth: ‘the servant ceaselessly draws near to Me by the supererogatory acts until I love him, and he loves Me.’ Then this other face of grace: the All-Merciful appoints for them a love in the hearts of His creatures and in the hearts of the angels; the people of good and obedience are loved by all, without having merited it by an act. Love (wudd) is doubly given: from the heart toward God, and from hearts toward them.

mots.racine: أ م ن / ع م ل / ص ل ح
mots.sens: those who believe and do good deeds
mots.def: ʿamila ṣāliḥāt: to do righteous deeds.

mots.racine: ج ع ل / ر ح م / و د د
mots.sens: the All-Merciful will appoint for them love

versets.ar: فَإِنَّمَا يَسَّرْنَاهُ بِلِسَانِكَ لِتُبَشِّرَ بِهِ الْمُتَّقِينَ وَتُنذِرَ بِهِ قَوْمًا لُّدًّا
versets.tr: fa-innamā yassarnāhu bi-lisānika li-tubashshira bihi l-muttaqīna wa-tundhira bihi qawman luddan
versets.trad: We have made it easy in your tongue, that you may give glad tidings by it to the godfearing and warn by it a contentious people.
versets.commentaire: Qushayrī: the word is one, the address is one; for one people it is facilitation, for another intimidation and warning. Blessed is he for whom it is made easy, by the grace that aids him; and woe to him whom it frightens, nay, whom it abandons! People are between the aided and the abandoned. The same Qurʾān divides hearts: glad tidings for the pious, warning for the contentious (ludd). The Word reveals what each one is before it.

mots.racine: ي س ر / ل س ن
mots.sens: We made it easy in your tongue
mots.def: yassara: to make easy; lisān: tongue.

mots.ar: لِتُبَشِّرَ بِهِ الْمُتَّقِينَ وَتُنذِرَ بِهِ قَوْمًا لُّدًّا
mots.tr: li-tubashshira bihi l-muttaqīna wa-tundhira bihi qawman luddan
mots.racine: ب ش ر / و ق ي / ن ذ ر / ل د د
mots.sens: to gladden the pious and warn the contentious

versets.ar: وَكَمْ أَهْلَكْنَا قَبْلَهُم مِّن قَرْنٍ هَلْ تُحِسُّ مِنْهُم مِّنْ أَحَدٍ أَوْ تَسْمَعُ لَهُمْ رِكْزًا
versets.tr: wa-kam ahlaknā qablahum min qarnin hal tuḥissu minhum min aḥadin aw tasmaʿu lahum rikzan
versets.trad: How many a generation We destroyed before them! Do you perceive a single one of them, or hear from them the faintest sound?
versets.commentaire: Seal of the sūra. Qushayrī: He established them and gave them life, fashioned and maintained them as He willed; then, when the moment came, He made them die and annihilated them. They perished all together, vanished to the last: neither great nor small, neither illustrious nor lowly — and they will be called to account, on the Day of resurrection, down to the least thread and the thinnest film. The silence (rikz, an imperceptible murmur) of the effaced generations is the last lesson: all passes, and no trace remains of those who thought themselves permanent — save the exact reckoning that will be demanded of them.

mots.racine: ه ل ك / ق ر ن
mots.sens: how many generations destroyed before them

mots.ar: هَلْ تُحِسُّ مِنْهُم مِّنْ أَحَدٍ أَوْ تَسْمَعُ لَهُمْ رِكْزًا
mots.tr: hal tuḥissu minhum min aḥadin aw tasmaʿu lahum rikzan
mots.racine: ح س س / س م ع / ر ك ز
mots.sens: do you perceive one of them, or hear a murmur?
mots.def: aḥassa: to perceive; rikz: a faint sound, murmur.
